revision.text:
{{pagebanner|ØsterbroBopaBanner.jpg|pgname=Østerbro}} '''Østerbro''' is a mainly residential district of [[Copenhagen]], just north of the [[Copenhagen/Indre By|inner city]]. With its quiet cobblestone and tree lined streets, and its excellent shopping and cafe life, it makes for a good excursion from the downtown area.

Along with [[Copenhagen/Frederiksberg|Frederiksberg]] in the west, Østerbro has long been considered the posh or upper class district of Copenhagen. These days this is mainly due to the demographic of young, liberal leaning, well paid academics, which has earned Østerbro nicknames like ''the Latté district'' and '''2100 Spelt'''. But it has been a neighborhood of the upper middle class since modern Østerbro's inception, when Copenhagen expanded beyond the old fortifications in the 1850s.
The district takes its name from being near the old eastern gate of the city, and translates roughly into the ''Eastern borough'', and before the city's defenses were dismantled, the area were largely dominated by grazing cows and grassy meadows, traces of which is still visible in the huge park; ''Fælledparken'', dominating the area. When Copenhagen was finally allowed to grow out over the ramparts following a cholera epidemic, Østerbro was not build up as quickly and disorganized as its neighbours Nørrebro and Vestebro, and it was mainly the bourgeoisie who moved here in pompous villas in green surroundings, a heritage which has stuck to the district, many of the mansions still stand today, and a lot of them are occupied by foreign embassies. When development finally took hold at the turn of the century, it was better planned, and more upscale than its counterparts, and with its big apartments and nicely crafted ornamented buildings. Even the construction of a new and dirty harbour by the waterfront and new factories could not quite take the bourgeoisie aura away from the district, something it has retained to this day. And while Østerbro is still trailing behind [[Copenhagen/Frederiksberg|Frederiksberg]] and its posh northern neighbour, [[Hellerup]] in terms of wealthy residents, visitors should still expect it to be fashionable, rather than "hip" or "cool", but watch out for the swarms of expensive prams parading the neighbourhood!
Nordhavn is a new development on the ground of the former North Harbour.
==Get in== ''Østerbro is almost entirely located within Zone 1 of the common ticketing system that covers both the Metro, Buses, and Trains in the Copenhagen area.''
=== By train === Østerbro is served both by the S-train suburban services that are grouped together through the central part of Copenhagen, The circle Metro line, as well as several regional lines that terminate on Østerbro's main station; Østerport. All S-Train lines except line F stop at the three S-stations on Østerbro: {{marker|type=go|name=Østerport|url=|lat=55.6928227|long=12.5878627}}, {{marker|type=go|name=Nordhavn|url=|lat=55.7056534|long=12.5907068}}, and {{marker|type=go|name=Svanemøllen|url=|lat=55.7155176|long=12.5789219}}, while lines A and F also have stops on {{marker|type=go|name=Ryparken|url=|lat=55.71507|long=12.55797}} station in the northern part of Østerbro. Svanemøllen and Ryparken stations are in Zone 2, so a northbound ticket might be cheaper from those. The Metro circle line have stops on {{marker|type=go|name=Trianglen|url=|lat=55.6992155|long=12.5755880}} and {{marker|type=go|name=Poul Hennings Plads|url=|lat=55.7094402|long=12.5763583}} === By bus ===
The backbone of bus transport in central Copenhagen are the A buses operated by [http://www.moviatrafik.dk/Service/Tourist/touristguideUK/Pages/TouristGuideUK.aspx Movia]{{Dead link|date=January 2023 |bot=InternetArchiveBot }}, which run every few minutes throughout the day. The S Buses are express buses that operate on longer lines, with fewer stops. '''Trianglen''' square connects five major streets on Østerbro and hosts bus stops for several lines. '''Trianglen''' can be recognized by its unusual building called ''the soup terrine'' by the locals.
*'''1A''' Starts at Avedøre station in the western Copenhagen, and runs through all major areas of downtown before entering Østerbro at Blegdamsvej and Trianglen before running along Østerbro's main street — Østerbrogade, to Svanemøllen station. The line terminates at Hellerup station. *'''3A''' This line does a half loop around downtown. Starting on Nordhavn station, and stopping at Trianglen, Rigshospitalet (the National hospital) and Panum university campus, before entering the [[Copenhagen/Nørrebro|Nørrebro]] district. *'''4A''' Begins at Svanemøllen station, and services the northern part of the district, before it crosses Lersøpark Allé to enter the [[Copenhagen/Nørrebro|Nørrebro]] district. It loops all around the city center, to terminate on [[Amager]]. *'''6A''' Starts in the suburb of Rødøvre west of Copenhagen, and passes the central station in downtown, before it enters Østerbro by ''the lakes'' with stops at the Rigshospitalet, the medical faculty of Copenhagen University, and the Jagtvej/Tagensvej intersection, before it enters the [[Copenhagen/North-West|North-West]] district and continues north. *'''150S''' Starts at Nørreport station in downtown, and runs through Østerbro with stops at the Rigshospitalet, Frederik Bajers Plads, The Østerbro university campus, and Ryparken station before continuing north along the highway to Kokkedal in northern Zealand.
=== By boat === [[Image:Suppeterrine cph.jpg|thumb|300px|The "soup terrine" building at Trianglen]] * [http://www.canaltours.com/dct/en Canal tours] '''Green line''' is waterbus that starts in the famous Nyhavn canal downtown, and stops at some of Østerbro's main attractions: the Little Mermaid, Langelinie, and Trekroner fortresses (summer only). Tickets are 60 Kr for a single journey, and 75 Kr for a one day ticket. Runs between 10:00-17:30 * The Arriva-operated '''901''' & '''902''' water buses are part of the public transportation system, and start at Nordre Toldbod, near the southern entrance of Kastellet, from where they cruise the inner harbour towards downtown. Prices and tickets are the same as on other buses and trains, but only if you use Rejsekortet or already have a valid ticket.
==Get around ==
You can reach most of Østerbro by foot, but as almost everywhere in Copenhagen, biking is really the best mode of transport. Bus 1A runs every 5 minutes and stops close to most attractions and places to eat and drink.
Østerbro is the area of all of Denmark that has the fewest parking spaces relative to the number of cars owned by residents, so it can be difficult to find a place to park a car after 16:00. And if you do find a place to park your car, think twice about using it if you will be returning in the evening.
==See== [[Image:TheLittleMermaid.jpg|thumb|250px|Swarms of tourists at the little mermaid statue]] [[Image:Kastellet aerial.jpg|thumb|250px|Kastellet]] While most visitors head straight to the top attraction, the little mermaid statue, they are mostly left baffled on how to react by the rather unremarkable tiny statue, but all is not lost. One tip is to actually read Hans Christians Andersen's fairy tale, the statue is meant to evoke the feeling of Ariel's longing and melancholy, not impress tourists. If you have not read the fairytale you can safely queue up with the tourists, get the ritual photo over and done with, and head for the other nearby attractions.
*{{see | name=Brumleby | alt= | url=http://www.brumleby.dk | email= | address= | lat=55.70163 | long=12.57556 | directions=The triangle between Øster Allé and Østerbrogade | phone=+45 35 43 14 08 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | wikidata=Q3432228 | content=Brumleby is a small quarter nestled in a triangle between Trianglen square and the National Stadium. It was built in the 1850s by the Danish Medical Association, as answer to a recent cholera epidemic, as a showcase on how to make healthy but affordable housing for the working class. It has been awarded Europa Nostra award for conservation, and it is a nice place for a brief stroll if you are in the area. }} :* {{see | name=Brumleby Museum | alt= | url=http://www.brumlebymuseum.dk/en/ | email= | address=Brumleby 134 (block N) | lat=55.7019168 | long=12.5756085 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= Sundays 12:00-15:00| price=DKK 25 | lastedit=2017-01-08 | content=Small museum in the old cooperative shop and library. It is run by volunteers living in Brumleby and they are happy to tell you everything about the museum and Brumleby. }} * {{see | name=Den Engelske Kirke | alt=St. Alban's - The English Church | url=http://www.st-albans.dk | email= | address=Churchill Parken 6 | lat=55.68919 | long=12.59699 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | hours= | price= | wikidata=Q3580820 | content=King Christian IX's daughter Alexandra, who was became the Queen of England was the driving force behind the erecting of this church, which is home to the English congregation in Denmark. The church was completed in 1887, and was built with some impressive flint stone walls. }} * {{see | name=Kastellet | alt=Frederikshavn Citadel | url= | email= | address= | lat=55.69137 | long=12.59447 | directions=a stone's throw away from Østerport station | phone=+45 33 47 95 00 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=24hrs daily | price=Free | wikipedia=Kastellet, Copenhagen | image=Kastellet Norgesporten Copenhagen.jpg | wikidata=Q221812 | lastedit=2017-11-28 | content=A superbly preserved and restored star-shaped citadel. First built in 1626 as St. Anne's Bulwark and completed in 1664, it was the stronghold at the northern end of the East Rampart, which arched through the city and remains in the form of the many parks extending down to Tivoli. The Citadel now houses the Danish Defense Intelligence Service, and is a popular spot for picnics. As well as two small museums with limited opening hours: Livjæger Museum and the museum in the North Gate which displays Garrison's Historical Collections . }} * {{see | name=Den lille Havfrue | alt=The Little Mermaid Statue | url= | email= | address=Langelinie | lat=55.692861 | long=12.599262 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | hours= | price= | wikidata=Q214619 | lastedit=2017-11-28 | content=This little lady is one of the most photographed in the world. Designed by ''Edvard Eriksen'', Her statue was erected in 1913 to commemorate a play of the Little mermaid. The poor lady has lost her head several times, and is often subject to vandalism connected with political statements. Many visitors are often surprised by her modest size (1.25 m (3.3 ft) high). The Little Mermaid is a semi official symbol of Copenhagen. }}
[[File:Nyboder Copenhagen.jpg|300px|thumb|Nyboder]]
* {{see | name=Nyboder Mindestuer | alt=& Nyboder district | url=http://www.nybodersmindestuer.dk/ | email= | address= | lat=55.6873712 | long=12.5880529 | directions=close to Østerport station | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Always open | price= | content=This mini-district was built in the 17th century, and consists of long rows of beautiful 2 story yellow brick buildings. It was built to house sailors from the Navy, as it was useful to have sailors in the fleet living in a confined space, so they could quickly be called to duty. Today the houses are still owned and managed by the Danish Defence, although servicemen from all three branches are allowed to rent a flat. All streets except for two are named after animals. On Sankt Pauls Gade 24, ''Nyboder mindestuer'' showcases everyday life in the neighborhood from its inception in 1631, every Sunday 11:00-14:00. }}
[[Image:Trekroner Seafortress Copenhagen 20090818 01.JPG|thumb|250px|Trekroner fortress]]
* {{see | name=Trekroner | alt=Trekroner fortress | url=http://www.trekronerfort.dk/ | email=info@fortet.dk | address= | lat=55.70293 | long=12.61363 | directions=12.61408 | phone=+45 38 25 44 44 | tollfree= | hours=Summer: 10:00-15:00 daily, closed the rest of the year | price=Admission is free, boat costs 35 kr | wikidata=Q1126084 | content=This 18th-century fort was built to guard the sea approach to Copenhagen's harbor in 1786. It has a special atmosphere, and some gorgeous views over Øresund. There are several well-preserved military buildings and fortifications. A restaurant and a café have been opened in a renovated building. The only access is via Canal Tour's green ''[https://www.stromma.com/en-dk/copenhagen/sightseeing/sightseeing-by-boat/hop-on-hop-off-boat/ hop on hop off]'' route (summer only). }}
* {{see | name=Gefion Fountain | alt= | url= | email= | address= | lat= | long= | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | hours= | price= | wikidata=Q952817 | lastedit=2026-03-01 | content=A large fountain featuring a large-scale group of oxen pulling a plow, driven by the Norse goddess Gefjon. }} === Museums === [[File:National_Gallery_of_Denmark_Walkway.jpg|alt=|thumb|444x444px|Walkway in National Gallery of Denmark]]
* {{see | name=Den Frie Udstilling | alt=The Free Exhibition | url=http://www.denfrie.dk | email=udstilling@denfrie.dk | address=Oslo Plads 3 | lat=55.6913902 | long=12.5874824 | directions=Opposite Østerport station | phone=+45 33 12 28 03 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=F-W 10:00-17:00, Th 10:00-21:00 | price=20-45 kr | content=This art exhibition was established in 1891, as a protest against a censored exhibition sponsored by the royal art board — hence the name. Mainly shows contemporary art. The exhibition is housed in a remarkable, restored building that resembles an ancient Greek temple. }} * {{see | name=Den Hirschsprungske Samling | alt=The Hirschprung Collection | url=https://hirschsprung.dk/en | email=dhs@hirschsprung.dk | address=Stockholmsgade 20 | lat=55.68993 | long=12.57767 | directions= | phone=+45 35 42 03 36 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=W-M 11:00-16:00 | price=85 kr, under 18 years free | wikipedia=Hirschsprung Collection | image=Hirschsprungske-samling.2004.jpg | wikidata=Q2982867 | lastedit=2017-11-28 | content=This collection was founded by the Copenhagen-born tobacco manufacturer Heinrich Hirschsprung (1836-1908), and moved into the current museum after his death in 1911. The museum hosts an impressive collection of Danish 19th-century art in a beautiful building displaying furniture from the artists homes. }} *{{see | name=Zoologisk Museum | alt=The Zoological museum | url=http://zoologi.snm.ku.dk | email= | address=Universitetsparken 15 | lat=55.70253 | long=12.55897 | directions= | phone=+45 35 32 22 22 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu-Su 10:00-16:00 | price=Joint ticket for both the Zoological Museum and the Geological Museum 95 Kr, students 50 kr | wikipedia=University of Copenhagen Zoological Museum | image=Zoologisk Museum.jpg | wikidata=Q220274 | lastedit=2017-11-28 | content=This museum is run by the University of Copenhagen. In the exhibitions, you can experience how the fauna changes as you travel from the North Pole to the South Pole, the Danish fauna since the Ice Age, including a 3 meters tall mammoth and the animals of the great oceans. The latest progress in science is brought to life in some very good temporary exhibitions. }} * {{see | name=Enigma | alt=Museum for Postal, Tele, and Communications | url=http://www.enigma.dk/ | email= | address=Øster Alle 1 | lat=55.6996298 | long=12.5751021 | directions=at Trianglen | phone=+45 33410900 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Th 11:00-21:00; F 08:00-18:00; Sa Su 10:00-16:00 | price=free | lastedit=2017-01-08 | content=So far only stage one of the new telecommunications museum is implemented. Which means that there is exactly one item on display, an old rusty Enigma machine. But it is free and they have a nice cafe and a museum shop with lots of expensive modern design. So if you are in the area it is worth a visit. }} * {{see | name=Statens Museum for Kunst (SMK) | alt=National Gallery of Denmark | url=http://www.smk.dk/en | email= | address=Sølvgade 48-50 | lat=55.68881 | long=12.57839 | directions= | phone=+45 33 74 84 94 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu-Su 11:00-17:00, W 11:00-20:00, M closed | price=Adults 110 kr, under 30 years 85 kr, under 18 years free | wikipedia=National Gallery of Denmark | image=Statens Museum for Kunst-side 1.jpg | wikidata=Q671384 | lastedit=2017-11-28 | content=Seven hundred years of Western art and cultural history lie under the roof of this striking Italian Renaissance building, which is itself situated in a nice park. Blockbusters include: Titian, Rembrandt, Picasso, Leger, Matisse and Emil Nolde. An array of other icons, paintings and sculptures are also on display. }} * {{See|name=Østerbro lille Museum|url=https://detlillemuseum.dk/|address=Nyborggade 9|lat=55.7098274|long=12.5784022|directions=in the basement|hours=F 11:00-15:00|price=free|lastedit=2022-06-03|content=small local history museum run by volunteers at the archives of local history.}}
==Do==
* {{do | name=Fiskerihavnen | alt= | url= | email= | address= | lat=55.72337 | long=12.60411 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=free | content=Located on the eastern tip of Nordhavn (North harbour). Because of the traffic on Øresund, commercial fishing is not allowed. But the amateur fishermen at Fiskerihavnen can fish from their small fishing boats. The fishermen have tiny sheds, all different, a bit messy, but colorful. To get there you have to zig-zag past Frihavnen (Freeport, closed to the public), mountains of containers, scrap metal, interesting industries. Bus 26 goes there. There is a store that sell fishing equipment, but also ice cream and cold beers. "Krølle" sells smoked herring and salmon from his 1914 boat, "Kællingen".
}} [[Image:CphFaelledparken.jpg|300px|thumb|Fælledparken]]
* {{do | name=Fælledparken | alt= | url= | email= | address= | lat=55.7019 | long=12.5690 | directions=Trianglen and Vibenhus Runddel metro stations are in opposite ends of the park. | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Fæleldparken is the big park behind the football stadium, the largest in Copenhagen. There is a lake, big trees and a pavilion with [http://www.cafepavillonen.dk/ a cafe] which hosts concerts during the summer, the outfit [http://www.salsabrava.dk/ Salsa Brava] also gives Salsa lessons in the park during the summer. Most of it is covered with grass, much of it marked as football fields. Go there for a game of football or another game, a picnic, or just lie in the grass. At May 1st, during the International Workers day, most of Copenhagen's population comes here in a peculiar mix of a political rally, and a seriously huge party. }} *{{do | name=Kaleidoskop K2 | alt=Krudttønden | url=http://www.krudttonden.dk/ | email= | address=Serridslevvej 2 | lat=55.7057152 | long=12.5748189 | directions= | phone=+45 35 42 83 62 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Hours vary | price=from 25 kr | content=Krudttønden is a cultural venue in Østerbro, with a mix of both concerts and theatrical performances, by both national and internationals acts. Also has a small café, that sometimes hosts various events. }} * {{do | name=Niels Bohr Institutet | alt= | url=http://www.nbi.dk | email=reception-blegdamsvej@nbi.dk | address=Blegdamsvej 17 | lat=55.69682 | long=12.57155 | directions= | phone=+45 35 32 52 09 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=You should make an appointment in advance. | price=Free | content=If you are a physicist or just interested in physics, come to the Institute and see the room of Niels Bohr with photos of the staff of the institute when he was the head of it, a model of the famous thought experiment weighing the clock, the archive, the photos of all the famous people who were developing the quantum theory during the so-called "[[golden age of physics]], when even small people could do big things" and also the modern part of the institute. The people at the archive are willing to show the institute to other people, if they are not too busy. }} * {{do | name=Park bio | url=http://www.parkbio-kbh.dk/ | email=parkbio@parkbio-kbh.dk | address=Østerbrogade 79 | lat=55.7037602 | long=12.5771638| directions= | phone=+45 35 38 33 62 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Usually 12:00-23:00 | price=65 kr | content=The only cinema on Østerbro, has an atmospheric lobby with tall plants, caged birds, and a café. Plays many Danish and European movies. }} * '''Football:''' {{do | name=Parken Stadium | alt= | url=http://www.parken.dk/ | email=info@parken.dk | address=Øster Allé 50 | lat=55.7027 | long=12.5724 | directions=near Trianglen | phone=+45 35 43 31 31 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | lastedit=2024-06-09 | content=Denmark's international soccer games are played at this 35,000-seat stadium. It's also the home ground of '''[https://www.fck.dk/ FC Copenhagen]''', who play in Superliga, the Danish top tier. It hosts major concerts, and has a gym, bowling alley and a pool. }} * '''[https://www.b93prof.dk/ B93]''' play in 1. Division, the second tier, at Østerbro Stadium (capacity 7000) next to Parken Stadium. * {{do | name=Østerbro Svømmehal | url=http://www.sport.kk.dk/obrohallenbad | email= | address=Gunnar Nu Hansens Plads 3 | lat=55.70449 | long=12.57661 | directions= | phone=+45 35 25 70 60 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 07:00-20:00, Sa Su 09:00-15:00 | price=30 kr for just the pool, 60 kr for all facilities | content=Pool, spa, aroma-bath, steam bath, and baby pool are all hosted in a beautifully restored 1929 building, somewhat resembling a Roman bath, topped with a large glass roof. It was the first public pool in build in the country. }} * {{do | name=Østre Gasværk | alt=Teatere | url=http://www.oestre-gasvaerk.dk/ | email= | address=Nyborggade 17 | lat=55.71010 | long=12.58185 | directions= | phone=+45 39 27 71 77 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=This theatre is located in a remarkable old gas storage container. Mainly hosts musicals but also has plays and comedy on the program. }} * {{do | name=Svanemølle Strand | url=http://www.kubik.kk.dk/svanemoellestrand | email= | address=Strandpromenaden | lat=55.7183908 | long=12.5836479 | directions=just north of Svanemølle harbor. You can walk there from Svanemølle station | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=24/7 but there are lifeguards 10:00-18:00 in the summer | price=free | content=Beach with a 130-m pier }} [[File:Göteborg Plads Sankej summer 2023.jpg|thumb|Göteborg Plads Sankej summer 2023]] * {{do | name=Sandkaj | alt= | url= | email= | address= | lat=/55.70630 | long=12.59464 | directions=In Nordhavn (Nordhavn metro Station) | phone= | tollfree= | hours= | price=free | lastedit=2023-07-09 | content=In the summer buy some cold beers or drinks in the nearby Lidl store and drink them on the wooden deck of Sandkaj, jump in the water when you get too warm in the sun. }}
==Buy==
Due to the young and rather wealthy demographic, there are many small stores catering the local crowd with designer clothes and home décor. There are also several small galleries and antique stores. These are mainly located on the main drags: Østerbrogade, Nordre Frihavnsgade and their side streets. On Saturdays year round there is an organic farmers market, Bondens Marked, on Jakobs Plads (Østebrogade). Groceries can be bought in ''MENY''' on Østerfælled Square which hosts a good delicatessen and a bakery. The Irma supermarket at Østerport station is convenient for travellers. * {{buy | name=Langelinie outlet | url=https://langelinie-outlet.dk/ | email= | address=Langeliniekaj | lat=55.69641 | long=12.60024| directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Daily 11:00-18:00 | price= | content=is a long shopping arcade, located on a promenade along the harbor, where the gigantic cruise ships dock. Several cafes, outlet stores, and souvenir shops (mainly catering to foreigners and cruise guests) have been beautifully fitted into the old stone bulwark. The ice cream stores at the end of the promenade are immensely popular with the locals during the summer. }} *{{buy | name=Moshi Moshi | alt= | url=https://www.moshimoshimind.com/ | email= | address=Dag Hammarskjölds Allé 40 | lat=55.6959717 | long=12.5804545 | directions= | phone=+45 35 38 70 78 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 11:00-18:00, Sa 11:00-15:00 | price= | content=This is actually 3 stores that are next to each other, and there is nothing here but the most unerringly stylish, minimal, eco-conscious women's clothing and shoes. }} *{{buy | name=Bahne | alt= | url= | email= | address=Østerbrogade 70 | lat=55.6991387 | long=12.5782943 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Interior decoration and houseware }} *{{buy | name=Rambow | url=https://www.rambow.dk/ | email= | address=Østerbrogade 55A | lat=55.7019275 | long=12.5771692| directions= | phone=+45 35 26 30 07 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 13:00-17:30 | price= | content=A small design boutique fitted into an old communal bathing institution, sells new and old furniture, kitchenwares, electronics from a host of national and (mostly) international designers and design bureaus. }}
==Eat==
===Budget===
*{{eat | name=Crazy Chicken | url= | email= | address=Rosenvængets Allé 1 | lat=55.698782 | long=12.578686 | directions= | phone=+45 35 26 41 42 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=10:00-10:22 | price=From 30 kr | content=This small place mainly serves as a take away place, but also has two tables available for a quick bite. Dishes out some excellent sandwiches and has menus with different sorts of chicken - as the name might imply - served with the option of several different Mediterranean inspired salads from a buffet (you get to choose 2) and/or potatoes. }}
There are some reasonable priced restaurants at the sailing clubs in Svanemøllen Harbour. For example: * {{eat | name=Cafe Sundet | alt= | url=http://cafesundet.dk/ | email=info@cafesundet.dk | address=Svaneknoppen | lat=55.7182331 | long=12.5910522 | directions=at the end of the north pier at Svanemølle harbour | phone=+45 39 29 30 35 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=May-Sept: M-F 11:30-21:00 (Kitchen 17:30-21:00), Oct-Apr: M W 16:00-22:00 | price=Mains from 128 kr | content=Located on the pier with a great view this place caters to sailors and passersby who just come to breathe in the harbor scene and ocean air. Kitchen is mainly Danish cuisine. }}
===Mid-range=== * {{Eat|name=Kafe Kapers|url=https://www.kafekapers.dk/|address=Gunner Nu Hansens Plads 2|lat=55.7050842|long=12.5768017|phone=+45 35251120|hours=09:30 to late|price=DKK 150 mains|content=Cozy cafe in a big glass cube on central Østerbro in the middle of the Gunner Nu Hansen square. Dogs are allowed in the pavilion section which is covered and heated in the winter}} * {{eat | name=Café Charlottehaven | alt= | url=https://www.charlottehaven.com/dk/en/gastronomy/food-and-drinks/ | email= | address=Hjørringgade 12C | lat=55.705692 | long=12.588267 | directions= | phone=+45 35271516 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 07:00-22;00, Sa Su 10:00-17:00 | price=The daily specials (M-F) at 125 kr are a good deal. Lunch from 85 kr, Weekend brunch 195 kr | content=Cafe/restaurant in one of the rare big rooms on Østerbro, for both your pocket, taste buds and your health. In the summer you can eat outside in the huge courtyard. Good selection of beer and wine, or you can try the fresh smoothies and lemonades. }} [[Image:Oesterbro soerne.jpg|thumb|300px|Den Franske Café and Guldanden by søerne at nighttime]] * {{eat | name=Dag H | alt=Café & Restaurant | url=https://www.dagh.dk/ | email= | address=Dag Hammarskjölds Allé 38 | lat=55.6958392 | long=12.5806623 | directions=Lille Triangel | phone=+45 35 27 63 00 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-W 08:00-23:00, Th F 08:00-midnight, Sa 10:00-midnight, Su 10:00-22:00 | price=3 course 389 kr | content=This café/restaurant is one of the first to open on Østerbro. The place offer early breakfast, brunch, a large and varied lunch menu. At the evening it transform into a restaurant where you can get a 3-course meal, their special Country Style Dish or try out their famous burger. Also a great place for a well made espresso, cocktail and they have much to offer on their wine menu. Last, many of the dishes you can also order to-go and get a discount. Main course from 195 Kr. }} * {{eat | name=Den Franske Cafe | alt=The French Cafe | url=https://denfranskecafe.dk/ | email= | address=Sortedams Dossering 101 | lat=55.6977025 | long=12.5779922 | directions= | phone=+45 35 42 48 45 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 09:00-22:00, Sa Su from 10:00 | price=From 98 kr | content=This café has outdoor tables by the lake under trees and coloured lanterns. The menu is mostly café classics like filled croissants, nachos, chilli con carne, pasta, burgers, but during the summer there is barbecue available until the late hours. }} * {{eat | name=Gourmanderiet | alt= | url=https://gourmandiet.dk/ | email= | address=Rosenvængets Allé 7A | lat=55.698971 | long=12.579424 | directions=close to Trianglen | phone=+45 39 27 10 00 | tollfree= | hours=Tu-F 11:00-19:00, Sa 09:00-15:00 | price=Mains from 120 kr, sandwiches and soups from 60 kr | content=Originally and in essence this is a high-end organic butchery, but an ever growing popularity with locals have prompted the owners to branch out, and offer a Brunch, Lunch and an evening "Dish of the Day", centred around their high quality meat from organic farms. Thursdays-Saturdays (17.50-midnight) they transform the place into a restaurant labeled Gourmanderiet by Night, and dish out fantastic steaks of every variety. }} * {{eat | name=Hos Fischer | url=https://hosfischer.dk/ | email= | address=Victor Borges Plads 12 | lat=55.701727 | long=12.580858 | directions= | phone=+45 35 42 39 64 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Daily 11:00-00:00 | price=Mains starts around 100 kr | content=Is a good, low key, Italian trattoria style restaurant. The menu varies greatly but if you can get hold of the Risotto, it is a treat. Otherwise go for one of the excellent pasta dishes. The Wine is very reasonably priced (for Copenhagen), and the staff is informal, but still attentive. }} * {{eat | name=Romarin | url=http://www.restaurant-romarin.dk/ | email= | address=Ryesgade 90 | lat=55.6962047 | long=12.5735838 | directions= | phone=+45 35 43 29 28 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Th 17:00-23:00, F Sa 17:00-23:30 | price=Main: 170 kr, 3 courses 240 kr | content=Good, though not outstanding, food at reasonable prices from the Italian and French kitchens, is made even more tolerable by the fact that you can freely bring your own wine and beer to enjoy with your food. Staff is relaxed in both the best and worst way, readily engaging in a hearty chat, but not terribly efficient. }} * {{eat | name=ØB | alt= | url=https://obbar.dk/ | email= | address=Østerbrogade 74 | lat=55.6995202 | long=12.5779994 | directions=at Trianglen | phone=+45 33113304 | fax= | hours=M-Th 16:00-24:00, F 14:00-01:00, Sa 12:00-01:00, Su 12:00-23:00 | price= 90 kr | lastedit=2023-07-09 | content=Beers and Burgers. }} * {{Eat|name=Silo|url=https://www.restaurantsilo.dk/|address=Helsinkigade 29, 17. floor|lat=55.7081109|long=12.5979465|phone=+45 39292920|hours=Th-Sa noon-23:00, Su 10:00-15:30|lastedit=2021-06-01 |content=Elegant food with a spectacular view over Copenhagen and the harbour. Do not miss the upstairs deck (18. floor), you can bring a drink from the cocktail bar up there. Or just visit Silo for a drink and the view |price=450 kr three course evening menu. }} * {{eat | name=Lupa| alt= | url=https://www.bistrolupa.dk/ | email= | address=Marstalsgade 8 | lat=55.7035059 | long=12.5851506 | directions=on Melchiors Plads | phone=+45 34 10 01 01 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=W-Fr 11:00-22:00, Sa 10:00-22:00 | price= 120 kr smallish mains. 400 kr three course menu | lastedit=2017-01-15 | content=Plant based bistro with a wine and cocktail bar. }} * {{eat | name=Brasserie Barner | alt= | url=https://brasseriebarner.dk/ | email= | address=Århusgade 1 | lat=55.7060768 | long=12.5782511 | directions=Østerbrogade and Århusgade | phone=+45 35264200 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=W-F 11:30-22:00, Sa 09:30-22:00, Su 09:30-21:00 | price= 200 kr mains | lastedit=2017-08-24 | content=Informal French food at reasonable prices. }} * {{eat | name=Restaurant Frihavn | alt= | url=https://restaurant-frihavn.dk/ | email= | address=Amerika Plads 28 | lat=55.7000434 | long=12.5940787 | directions= | phone=+45 21959503 | tollfree= | hours=M-Sa 11:30-17:30 | price=90 kr lunch dishes. Most people can eat two | lastedit=2020-02-13 | content=Traditional lunch in beautiful 1895 former half-timer freeport station building }} *{{Eat|name=Madklubben Østerbro|url=https://madklubben.dk/en/restaurants/madklubben-oesterbro/|lat=55.7040023|long=12.5771702|directions=Trianglen metro station|phone=+45 38414173|hours=Tu-W 17:00-23, Th-Sa 17:00-24:00|price=Three course menu 350 kr|lastedit=2022-06-04|content=Good food at reasonable prices in iconic 1920'ies building.}}
===Splurge=== * {{eat | name=Geranium | alt= | url=http://www.geranium.dk/ | email= | address=P.H. Lings Alle 4 | lat=55.7037103 | long=12.5720401 | directions=nope, your GPS is not drunk, it's inside the stadium | phone=+45 69 96 00 20 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Booking is essential | price=Set menu for 1048 kr, wines double the price | wikipedia=Geranium (restaurant) | wikidata=Q15573853 | lastedit=2020-03-01 | content=Reopened in new surroundings by chef Rasmus Kofoed who won the 2011 Bocuse d'Or, and is one of the best chefs in the world. The restaurant got three Michelin stars in 2016. The restaurant is along with Noma at the forefront of the revolution in Nordic cuisine that is current waging on. Danish newspapers and guides all agree; if you want to sample local gourmet, this is a very good place to be seated. }} * {{eat | name=Le Saint Jacques | url=http://www.danielletz.com/HTML/daniel_letz_lesaintjacques.html | email=lsj@danielletz.com | address=Sankt Jakobs Plads 1 | lat=55.703152 | long=12.577764 | directions= | phone=+45 35 42 77 07 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Su-W 11:00-00:00, Th-Sa 11:00-02:00 | price=Mains from 180 kr | content=Very French kitchen — brasserie style with white walls, candlelights, Russian icons and more religious symbols than your average church. With the quirky décor, good wines, and the style of the buildings on the square, it does not take much effort to imagine yourself in Paris when dining here. }} * {{eat | name=Gro Spiseri | alt= | url=https://www.grospiseri.dk/ | email= | address=Æbeløgade 4 | lat=55.7112065 | long=12.5644201 | directions=Through the port, up the spiral stair to the roof. | phone=+45 50368147 | tollfree= | hours=Th-M. There are two sittings: 17:30 and 20:30. Book well in advance. | price=6-course menu, 500 kr | lastedit=2020-02-14 | content=Guests sit close at the long table in the small greenhouse on rooftop farm, so you will get to know some of the locals. The food is based on Østergros own organic production and matched with natural wines. The staff will be happy to discuss the food, Østergro, rooftop farming, community supported agriculture, etc. There is only one menu, but you can request vegan, vegetarian, etc. }}
==Drink== Østerbro is not the place for raving nightlife, apart the Office and partly Café Bopa. Most places on Østerbro are low key, cozy places where you go to have a ''hyggelig'' conversation with friends, over a few snacks and beers or wine with quiet music playing in the background. Most locals head downtown for a big night out. As is the case with almost every bar in Copenhagen, expect draft beers to set you back 40-50 Kr.
* {{drink | name=Cafe Bopa | alt= | url=https://cafebopa.dk/ | email= | address=Bopa Plads 1 | lat=55.7050954 | long=12.5812779 | directions= | phone=+45 35 43 05 66 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Su-W 10:00-midnight, Th 10:00-02:00, F Sa 10:00-05:00 | price= | content=A café during daylight, this place morphs into a crowded bar with DJs spinning records during the weekends. Relaxed atmosphere and great for socializing }} *{{drink | name=Pixie | alt= | url=https://www.cafe-pixie.com/ | email= | address=Løgstørgade 2 | lat=55.704993 | long=12.581196 | directions=next to Café Bopa, east of Østerbrogade at Gunner Nu Hansens Plads | phone=+45 39 30 03 05 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Su-Th 09:30-01:00, F Sa 09:30-03:00 | price= | content=hands some reasonably priced cocktails (a flat 65 kr) over the counter, Outdoor seating on the small square, and brunch, lunch, tapas and an evening menu if you want to fill up your stomach before indulging. Pixie is the name for a series of well known children's books, and the owners have kept true to the name with pretty neat colorful childish interiors. }}
[[Image:amokka cph.jpg|thumb|300px|Cafe DagH on Østerbro]]
* {{drink | name=Dag H | alt= | url=https://www.dagh.dk/ | email= | address=Dag Hammarskjölds Alle 36 | lat=55.6958392 | long=12.5806623 | directions= | phone=+45 35 27 63 00 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-W 08:00-23:00, Th-Sa 08:00-midnight, Su 10:00-midnight | price=Brunch:125 kr, mains: 169 kr | content=Located on a small square, colloquially known as the little Triangle, this cafe is popular for its huge outdoor serving area, at the end of ''the lakes'', it is a bit overpriced, but the coffee is top-notch and they have a decent collection of Danish and foreign beers. }} *{{drink | name=Docken | alt= | url=https://www.docken.dk/ | email= | address=Færgehavnsvej 35 | lat=55.718631 | long=12.5975932 | directions= | phone=+45 39 29 92 00 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Su-Th 11:00-23:00, F Sa 11:00-midnight. Early June to late August | price= | content=With a huge outdoor area and an artificial beach, it is perfect for chilling on warm days. But they also house a restaurant, and the two large raw industrial storage buildings, houses exhibitions and concerts all summer long. }} * {{drink | name=Tap 10 | alt= | url= | email= | address=Østerbrogade 122 | lat=55.7049052 | long=12.5776539 | directions= | phone=+45 61724487 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | lastedit=2016-11-04 | content=Small cosy place that has 10 interesting beers on tap and make good cocktails. }} * {{drink | name=Bubbles | alt= | url=http://bubbles-cph.dk/ | email= | address=Århusgade 14 | lat=55.705922 | long=12.578952 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | hours= | price= | lastedit=2017-08-24 | content=Champagne and cocktail bar. }} * {{drink | name=Strandlyst | alt= | url= | email= | address=Nygårdsvej 1 | lat=55.712697 | long=12.576924 | directions=South of Svanemøllen station | phone=+45 27207169 | tollfree= | hours=Tu-Su 12:00-24:00 | price=20 kr beers | lastedit=2019-01-16 | content=Old-fashioned brown Danish bar with cheap beers, a digital jukebox, dice-games. But non-smoking. }}
==Sleep== The options for staying at Østerbro are rather limited, as most hotels in Copenhagen are located in the [[Copenhagen/Indre By|downtown]] area — most of the surrounding neighborhoods, including Østerbro, are mainly residential. An alternative, often offering better value for money, are the numerous bed & breakfasts in private apartments.
===Hotels=== * {{sleep | name=Rye115 Hotel | alt= | url=https://rye115.com | email=info@rye115.com | address=Ryesgade 115 | lat=55.6977417 | long=12.5764189 | directions= | phone=+45 31686245 | tollfree= | fax= | checkin=14:00 | checkout=11:00 | price=From 700 kr single |lastedit=2019-03-25| content=This is a small local boutique hotel with a cozy atmosphere serving homemade organic breakfast, feels like a real apartment. }} * {{sleep | name=Østerport Hotel | alt= | url=http://hotelosterport.dk/en/ | email=reception@hotelosterport.dk | address=Oslo Plads 5 | lat=55.6917799 | long=12.5869417 | directions=at Østerport station right at the tracks (it is soundproofed) | phone=+45 70 12 46 46 | tollfree= | fax= | checkin=14:00 | checkout=12:00 | price=Singles from 675 kr if booked online | content=really central with most trains in and out of Copenhagen just a minute away, direct trains to the airport, it is close to most of Østerbro's main attractions, and right at the outskirts of [[Copenhagen/Indre By|downtown]]. }} * {{Sleep|name=Stay Seaport|url=https://stayapartments.dk/seaport/|address=Murmanskgade 15|lat=55.7082450|long=12.5987369|phone=+45 70707586|lastedit=2021-06-01 |content=Modern hotel in North Harbour. |directions=Nordhavn is the closest Metro }} * {{sleep | name=Comwell Copenhagen Portside | alt= | url=https://comwell.com/en/hoteller/copenhagen-portside | email= | address= | lat=55.71281 | long=12.5939 | directions=At Orientkaj Metro station |price=900 kr | phone=+45 70274274 | tollfree= | hours= | lastedit=2021-06-01 | content=Modern spacious hotel in the North Harbour area. }} === Apartment rentals === Private apartment rentals and semi-bed & breakfasts are by large the main type of accommodations on Østerbro. These are often spacious and classic apartments from the 19th century, with high, decorated ceilings, and huge windows facing the street—as is the norm on Østerbro.
==Connect== The postal code for Østerbro is 2100 (''København Ø'')
* {{listing | name=Østerbro Library | alt=Østerbro Bibliotek | url=http://www.bibliotek.kk.dk/english | email=biboes@kff.dk | address=Dag Hammarskjölds Alle 19 | lat=55.694588 | long=12.5817704 | directions= | phone=+45 35 38 16 48 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Th 10:00-19:00, F 12:00-18:00, Sa 10:00-15:00 | price=Free | content=Has 11 computers available for internet surfing, it is possible to book a PC online at library's website. }}
== Cope == * {{listing | name=The Laundromat Café | alt= | url=http://www.thelaundromatcafe.com/ | email= | address=Århusgade 38 | lat=55.7060620 | long=12.5806697 | directions= | phone=+45 35 55 60 20 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Th 08:00-23:00, F Sa 10:00-00:00 | price=32 kr for laundry including detergent | content=Along with its sister cafe on [[Copenhagen/Nørrebro#Cope|Nørrebro]], this bar and café is a perfect match for a traveler. Combining a coin laundromat with a café where you can sit and enjoy a coffee, beer, lunch or brunch, while reading one of the 3000 paperback titles or abusing the free wireless internet, and waiting for your laundry to finish. }} * {{listing | name=Rigshospitalet | alt=The National hospital | url=http://www.rigshospitalet.dk/ | email=rigshospitalet@rh.regionh.dk | address=Blegdamsvej 9 | lat=55.6959762 | long=12.5666504 | directions= | phone=+45 35 45 35 45 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Although the largest and most important Hospital in the country, this hospital's ER only takes in patients arriving by ambulance, or referred by a doctor. This is due to large number of patients preferring to be hospitalized here, rather than one of the other Copenhagen Hospitals. Should you require hospitalization, patients on Østerbro are referred to Bispebjerg hospital in the [[Copenhagen/North-West#Cope|North-West]] district. }} * {{listing | name=Trianglen Apoteket | alt=Pharmacy | url=http://www.apoteket.dk/Apotek.aspx?id=1287 | email=trianglen@apoteket.dk | address=Nordre Frihavnsgade 3 | lat=55.6996138 | long=12.5783952 | directions= | phone=+45 35 38 32 00 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 09:00-17.30 except W 09:00-18:00, Sa 09:00-14:00 | price= | content=Large and well stocked chemist. }}

{{geo|55.6993|12.5775}}

{{pagebanner|Fristaden Christiania graffiti (17181492918) (cropped).jpg|pgname=Christiania}} : ''Christiania is the former name of [[Oslo]]'' '''Christiania''' is a self-governing "free town" established in 1971, a city within the city, on the site of a military barracks as an alternative to mainstream culture. Christiania is considered to be the fourth largest tourist attraction in Copenhagen.
==Understand == [[Image:Stadsgraven - Christiania.jpg|thumb|300px|One of the best views in town]] [[Image:Christiania.jpg|thumb|right|300px|A view over the fortification canal running through Christiania]] Christiania is an intentional community and commune of about 850 to 1,000 residents, covering 7.7 hectares (19 acres) in the borough of Christianshavn in Copenhagen on the island of Amager.
The open trade of cannabis was shut down in April 2024 as a collaborative effort with police and the majority of the inhabitants of Christania.
===Local information === * [https://www.christiania.org Christiania local government] website
==Get in== Christiania is car-free, and parking conditions outside can be hellish in good weather, so do what everyone else does and jump on a bike, if that doesn't cut it, take public transit; there are two options:
* Take the '''metro''' to Christianshavn station, from there it is about a 6-minute walk (500 m), when you exit the metro station continue straight ahead (south) until the second intersection (there is a furniture store on the corner) turn left down (east) ''Prinsessegade'' and walk a couple of hundred meters, you won't miss it, if you have any sort of idea what you are looking for. * You can also bus opt for '''bus''' 9A, which stops right outside the entrance, it leaves from both Vesterport and the central station, about every 10 minutes for most of the day.
== See == {{Mapframe|55.67703|12.60536|zoom=15|width=450|height=550}} {{Mapmask |55.6734,12.5959 |55.6745,12.5977 |55.6746,12.5987 |55.6754,12.5998 |55.6745,12.6020 |55.6744,12.6027 |55.6744,12.6037 |55.6746,12.6043 |55.6751,12.6051 |55.6770,12.6079 |55.6789,12.6093 |55.6795,12.6096 |55.6815,12.6101 |55.6823,12.6101 |55.6823,12.6108 |55.6836,12.6134 |55.6833,12.6135 |55.6831,12.6143 |55.6827,12.6144 |55.6824,12.6138 |55.6804,12.6137 |55.6800,12.6143 |55.6795,12.6143 |55.6793,12.6133 |55.6774,12.6125 |55.6767,12.6128 |55.6764,12.6125 |55.6762,12.6115 |55.6754,12.6109 |55.6747,12.6098 |55.6741,12.6099 |55.6738,12.6094 |55.6738,12.6087 |55.6729,12.6074 |55.6718,12.6046 |55.6712,12.6044 |55.6713,12.6033 |55.6716,12.6020 |55.6714,12.6009 |55.6708,12.6002 |55.6708,12.5994 |55.6711,12.5987 |55.6717,12.5988 |55.6722,12.5987 |55.6734,12.5959}}
There are hundreds of buildings on Christiania, in all sorts of imaginative shapes, sizes and colours ranging from old army buildings dating back to the by-gone barrack days, to elaborate self-build homes. The best way to see them is just walking around sucking in the atmosphere. There is graffiti art everywhere, too. All the buildings have their own story. If you are friendly, open-minded, a bit lucky, and outside the main tourist season, you can sometimes get locals to tell you the, often amazing, stories of their particular house; otherwise the regular walking tours are a good introduction to the main sights.
[[File:Christiania map.jpg|thumb|]]
{{Infobox|Smoke-away?|The most famous tourist draw to Christiania is no longer there, but rather on the National Museum in [[Copenhagen/Indre By|downtown]]. The old colourful hash-stalls which used to line Pusher street, was demolished in 2004, in an attempt to gain permanent rights to occupy the area from the right wing government. One of them, the signature blue "Ryg med hjem (Smoke-away)" booth, was salvaged, and is now on display in the modern collection of the National Museum, complete with furnace, sales tables and instruments to sell marijuana, but without the authentic goods. [[Image:Cph Hashbod.jpg|center|200px]]}} * {{see | name=Fredens Ark | alt=Ark of Peace | url= | email= | address=Fredens Eng | lat=55.67287 | long=12.59827 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | image=Fredens ark christiana.jpg | content=The Ark is the largest building on Christiania, and the largest half-timbered house in Northern Europe. It was built in 1837 as the main building of the barracks, with sleeping and officers quarters. Today around 80 people live in the protected building, and the lower levels are home of the 'Rockmaskinen' club which has occational events and the community's social counseling (Herfra og videre) }} * {{see | name=Mælkebøtten | alt=The Dandelion | url= | email= | address=Langgaden | lat=55.6741 | long=12.6054 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=This cluster of buildings a good walk up Langgade, dates back to the middle of 19th century, and used to be the laboratory of the country department (Landetaten). The impressive gate-building is probably the finest of the old buildings on Christiania, and the wonderful contrast of the Buddhist Stupa in front makes a good photo, above the gate is King Christian IIV's gold monogram. The whole complex is protected. }} * {{see | name=Vilhelms Bastion | alt=Børneengen | url= | email= | address=Nordområdet | lat=55.67604 | long=12.60823 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=One of the old bastions of the fortification on which Christiania is built, and has a set of protected buildings. '''Kommandørhuset''' is a pretty, yellow, half timbered house which used to serve as a guard building, the eave on the eastern side was extended to allow for the soldiers to stay dry during rain. It was supposedly built and constructed by an Italian architect. Another protected building here is the '''Krudthuset''', the oldest building on the mound from 1665, which used to be a gunpowder store but is now used as a residence. The area is now used for many activities for the local kids, with a riding school, stables and playgrounds. }}
[[Image:Christiania Street.JPG|thumb|250px|Cameras away, you've reached Pusher Street]]
* {{see | name=Gallopperiet | alt=Stadens Museum for Kunst, Christiania Art Museum | url=http://www.gallopperiet.dk | email= | address=Sydområdet | lat=55.6738 | long=12.5969 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu-Su 14:00-19:00 | price= | content=A pun to the much more conformist "Statens Museum for Kunst" in downtown, Stadens (which is the colloquial name for Christiania) Museum for kunst is a gallery and book café with changing art exhibitions. It's in the 2nd floor of the Loppen building which was a military storage building when it was constructed back in 1863. A good call, since the quite large and rustic exbition space, is a really pleasant place to breeze through. }} ** {{see | name=Galleri Leonard | alt= | url= | email= | address=Sydområdet | lat= | long= | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Variable opening hours | price= | content=Painter's workshop and exhibition in the ground floor of the "Loppen" building. It's run by a local artists, and sometimes a makeshift stage for small concerts. }}
==Do== Christiania is something of a powerhouse in Copenhagen's cultural life, especially on the alternative scene, and there will almost always be something going on, in one of the many club and venues dotted around the central part near Pusher street, or the open air scene on Café Nemoland (listed under drink). One of the most popular activities is simply buying some weed and consuming it at a chill spot.
[[Image:Cph-Christiana-beach.jpg|thumb|250px|Christiania's beach]]
* {{do | name=Alis in Wonderland | url= | email= | address=Prærieområdet | lat=55.6737 | long=12.5980 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=From humble beginnings with a small skate ramp founded by a skater-oriented community on Pusher Street (ALIS), this skate ramp - crafted by local skaters who were invited to build whatever they wanted after their old ramp was forced to relocate from noise complaints. The efforts turned into roll-ins, pool corners and oververts, and after a harsh winter took its toll with the ramp was in dire straits, friends were called back in - and many hours later the ramp was indoor, with inside and outside balconies and an exotic atmosphere with Hawaiian paintings and hammocks. The outside 'Wonderland' mural drawing from the familiar fairytale is an attraction in its own right. }} * {{do | name=Christiania Jazzclub | alt= | url=https://twitter.com/christianiajazz?lang=en | email=info@christiania-jazzclub.com | address=Mælkevejen | lat=55.6738 | long=12.5993 | directions=the 2nd building on your left after you enter Pusher street | phone= | tollfree= | hours=W F 21:00-late | price= | content=This Jazz club, housed in Christiania's children's theatre, has an amazing atmosphere. If you play any instruments, often you can go to stage and jam away at the late hours of the night after the band of the night have finished. }} *{{do | name=Den Grå Hal | alt=The Grey Hall | url= | email= | address=Refshalevej 2 | lat=55.6747 | long=12.6006 | directions=turn left at the entrance to Pusher street | phone=+45 21 22 98 74 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=Price varies | image=| wikidata=Q12308060 | content=Christiania's biggest cultural venue with room for 1500 people, 'Den Grå Hal' is something of an authority on the Danish music scene, and despite its somewhat modest appearance, has hosted some big stars in music such as Metallica, Bob Dylan and Rage Against the Machine. There are other events like markets, theatre or parties. Events are usually listed on Christiania's website. }} * {{do | name=Loppen | url=http://www.loppen.dk/ | email=loppen@loppen.dk | address=Sydområdet 4B | lat=55.6741 | long=12.5973 | directions= | phone=+45 32 57 84 22 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=It is a rock club where various alternative bands play live. The room in the old artillery storage with low ceilings and sturdy dark wooden support pillars can fit up to 450 people. The low stage takes you face to face with the bands playing, creating an intimate atmosphere, much appreciated by local bands who often forgo bookings at bigger stages elsewhere to play there. }} * {{do | name=Energiværkstedet | alt=the Energy workshop | url= | email=larsewe@mail.dk | address=Mælkevejen | lat=55.6734 | long=12.6005 | directions= | phone=+45 20 84 87 12 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Part of a local association working with spiritual energies: Yoga, T'ai Chi Chuan, Aikido, massage, Shiatsu, body consciousness etc. There are a lot of lectures going on, but at no particular schedule except for regular Tai Chi sessions. }} * {{do | name=The Opera | alt=Operaen | url=http://www.operaen.net/ | email= | address=Gaden | lat=55.6737 | long=12.5994 | directions= | phone=+45 32 57 29 09 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=The Opera on Christiania, not to be mistaken by the more conventional Operahouse nearby, is a combined music venue, restaurant and bar, with a wide variety of bands playing. Hosts a blues night on Sundays. }}
==Buy==
Christiania offers some truly unique shopping, apart from its infamous major export item: [[cannabis]]. If you want to bring home a really unique souvenir from Copenhagen, there is a bunch of interesting options here. As of August 2024, Pusher Street, where you could buy everything from cannabis posters to bongs and Jamaican clothing, has been de-paved, and is no more, though.
Despite efforts by the police to shut down the cannabis trade here, the selling is still going on relatively openly. The cannabis market in Copenhagen is estimated to around 1 billion kroner (€134 million) annually, and with Christiania still being the epicentre, neither is there much chance of the trade disappearing any time soon. But potential buyers should be aware that police occasionally patrol the area, and you could be fined if you are caught possessing hashish. Harder drugs are however frowned upon on Christiania, and requesting them is a ''faux pas''.
*{{buy | name=Christiania Bikes | url=http://www.christianiabikes.dk/ | email= | address=Mælkevejen 83A | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+45 32 54 87 48 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 09:00-17:00, Sa 10:00-14:00 | price= | content=Produces some unique handcrafted bikes, their signature bike has a boot in the front for goods, children or groceries. }} *{{buy | name=Indkøbscentralen | alt= | url=https://www.facebook.com/Indk%C3%B8bscentralen-Cristiania-143513142371852/ | email= | address= | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+45 32 96 15 51 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=W-M 08:00-20:00, Tu 12:00-20:00 | price= | content=This store sells mostly organic groceries, some produced locally, e.g. organic cosmetics }} *{{buy | name=Qsmedien | alt=The woman ironworks | url=http://kvindesmedien.dk/ | email= | address=Mælkevejen 83 E | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+45 32 57 76 58 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 09:00-17:00, Sa 11:00-15:00 | price= | content=An iron workshop run by 3 women, mostly selling interior decorations and furniture cast of iron and glass. }}
==Eat== With some of the best vegetarian food you'll find anywhere in town, the residents love for everything organic, and the social responsibly (and socialism) so enshrined in the fabric of Christiana culture, that prices are kept at quite reasonable levels — especially considering the quality, Christiania is an excellent place for lunch and dinner. Often there is an open barbecue up on Pusher street dishing some yummy burgers and hot dogs.
*{{eat | name=Månefiskeren | alt=Moonfisher | url=https://www.christiania.org/find/manefiskeren/ | email= | address= | lat=55.6735 | long=12.6014 | directions=just behind Pusher St | phone=+45 3257 1456 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=10:00-00:00 | price= | content=Relaxed café with room for both Christianites and people dropping by for a cup of coffee, a game of table football or billiards. Has some decent sandwiches and good cakes from time to time, but does not serve alcohol as joints are welcome. }} *{{eat | name=Morgenstedet | alt= | url=http://www.morgenstedet.dk | email= | address=Fabriksområdet 134 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu-Su 12:00-21:00 | price=Mains from {{DKK|70}} | image=Morgenstedet (Christiania).jpeg | wikidata=Q12327614 | content=Morgenstedet is a vegetarian eatery, and all the food here is made from organic and fresh vegetables from a Danish farm. Nice outdoor seating along benches in the garden, and nice rustic interiors inside. The service is can be really laid back, and the place is mostly self-service, but the open kitchen and talkative chefs make up for it. }} *{{eat | name=Spiseloppen | url=http://www.spiseloppen.dk/ | email= | address= | lat=55.6738 | long=12.5969 | directions= | phone=+45 32 57 95 58 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu-Su 17:00-22:00 | price=Mains from {{DKK|165}} | content=A nice restaurant where the menu changes daily, and according to what fresh foods are available during the different seasons. There is always a fish and a vegetarian dish on the menu, and the ethnic mix in the kitchen usually ensures an amazing variety throughout the week. }} *{{eat | name=Café Nemoland | alt= | url=https://nemoland.dk/ | email= | address=Fabriksområde 52 | lat=55.67282 | long=12.6002 | directions=by the end of Pusher street | phone=+45 3295 8931 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=09:00 - 21:00 (in summer until 22:00) | price= | content=This lovely place is part outdoor beer garden, part concert venue, part indoor café, with possibility to eat outside. Creative decoration, and the most varied crowd you are likely to see anywhere in Copenhagen. }} *{{eat | name=Sunshine Bakery | url=https://www.christiania.org/find/sunshine-bakery/ | email= | address=Tinghuset 44E | lat=55.6736 | long=12.5992 | directions=cnr Pusher St | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=24 hr | price= | content=A kiosk and bakery hybrid with bread, cakes and sandwiches, coffee, tea, dairy products, soft drinks, cigarettes, etc., both for take away, or enjoying at the outdoor benches in front of the bakery. }}
==Drink== [[Image:Cph-Nemoland.jpg|thumb|200px|Café Nemoland]] *{{drink | name=Café Nemoland | alt= | url=https://nemoland.dk/ | email= | address=Fabriksområde 52 | lat= | long= | directions=by the end of Pusher St | phone=+45 3295 8931 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=10:00-02:00 (weekends until 03:00) | price= | content=This lovely place is part outdoor beer garden, part concert venue, part indoor café. Creative decoration, and the most varied crowd you are likely to see anywhere in Copenhagen. }} *{{drink | name=Woodstock | url= | email= | address=Pusher Street | lat= | long= | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=09:00-17:00 | price= | content=A pub with occasional live music. Breakfast, sandwiches, beer, soft drinks, alcohol, coffee and tea. }} *{{drink | name=Månefiskeren | alt=Moonfisher | url=http://home.christiania.org/~moon/ | email= | address= | lat= | long= | directions=just behind Pusher street in Christiania | phone=+45 3257 1456 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=10:00–00:00 | price= | content=Relaxed café. Has some decent sandwiches and cakes, but does not serve alcohol. Many organic available, and joints are welcome. }}
==Sleep== There is no public option for sleeping, unless you bump into someone willing to lend you a couch. The nearest hotels are in [[Copenhagen/Amager|Amager]] or [[Copenhagen/Indre By|Downtown]]
==Stay safe== This area presents a dramatic shift of mood from the rest of Copenhagen. Cannabis is being openly sold and consumed throughout Christiania, especially on Pusher Street, so be aware of that if you decide to bring children.
Some of the cannabis sellers are employed by gangs. Do not take photographs in or near Pusher Street, or you will be confronted by a local pusher or gang member, with threats of violence. None of them wants to be identified by the police, which means they have to keep a low profile. They keep an eye out for policemen or journalists working undercover. That said, if you respect the nature of this unusual environment and its rules, you should be okay. Exercise common sense by keeping an eye on your surroundings and belongings, and keep a low profile.
No matter how relaxed Christiania might seem, possession and sale of cannabis are still illegal in Denmark. While the police very rarely manage to make their way into Christiania proper, those wishing to take cannabis out with them should be careful: if you don't want to risk arrest, don't bring anything illegal outside the Free State's borders.
==Respect== You won't find any rules carved in stone or typed-up in bylaws, but Christiania has been around for some time, and they don't tolerate some things. The following rules are generally accepted by everyone:
* No cars are allowed in Christiania * No weapons are allowed in Christiania (this includes defensive items like pepper spray and bullet-proof vests) * No hard drugs are allowed in Christiania (e.g., opiates, cocaine, ecstasy) * Do not take photos inside the Green Light District (Pusher Street) – this is where weed was openly sold. * Be respectful. Many people live in Christiania. It's open to visitors, but never forget that you're their guest.
{{guidedistrict}} {{IsPartOf|Copenhagen}} {{geo|55.6735889|12.5997111}}

revision.text:
{{pagebanner|Christianshavn view from Vor Frelsers Kirke Wikivoyage banner.png|pgname=Christianshavn|caption=View from the Vor Frelsers Kirke belltower over Christianshavn, looking towards the Indre By}} '''Christianshavn''' is a district east of the inner city of [[Copenhagen]]. Once a working-class neighbourhood, today Christianshavn is a trendy part of the city with its own unique identity. people from all walks of life, live side-by-side among cool cafes, old school bodegas, fancy restaurants and charming 19th-century houses along picturesque canals. Further north, '''Holmen''' is an old naval area, that has seen a dramatic change in the past few years, with the old buildings being updated for residence, along with new dramatic architecture - fittingly a new home of the royal Danish school of architecture.
[[File:Stadsgraven - house and spire.jpg|thumbnail|right|Stadsgraven]] ==Understand== Christianshavn is an artificial island, erected in the swampy area between what is now the [[Copenhagen/Indre_By|Inner city]] and the island of [[Amager]], by King Christian the 4th, as a grand unification of his mad dash of construction projects, and his ambitions to develop [[Denmark]] into a major Naval power.
The district was built as part of the southern fortification of Copenhagen in the early 17th century. It was designed in the image of Amsterdam, with a canal cutting straight through the centre — from this point Danish merchants were to expand trade over the seven seas, a heritage that is still very visible today, with the forest of masts from the sailing ships docked along the canal, and the century-old watering holes of sailors still handing out beers and ''snaps'' to thirsty passers by.
In the early days Christianshavn was an independent city, until it was swallowed by the rapidly expanding capital, but it retains a unique atmosphere — and the people refer to themselves at Christianhavners first, and then as Copenhageners. In many ways Christianshavn is still a small town in a big city. Diversity is thriving here, and the district has managed to embrace people from all walks of life; from the alcoholic sipping beer at the corner, to the marketing yuppie, and form its own special identity stemming from this diversity.

|55.6685,12.5828 |55.6682,12.5840 |55.6677,12.5878 |55.6676,12.5904 |55.6684,12.5957 |55.6687,12.5963 |55.6691,12.5959 |55.6697,12.5991 |55.6696,12.6002 |55.6701,12.6006}}
Christianshavn and the subdistrict of Holmen are on a peninsula stretching out north through the old fortifications. Because it is virtually surrounded by water, the only access is across the Knippelsbro Bridge and the dam to [[Amager]]. It is entirely located within Zone 1 of the common ticketing system of the Metro, trains and buses. If you prefer not to pay for mass transit, it is a pleasant 10-minute walk from Strøget on Amagertorv, past the parliament, and over the Knippelsbro Bridge.
=== By bus === The backbone of bus transport in central Copenhagen are the A buses operated by [http://www.moviatrafik.dk/ Movia], which run every few minutes throughout the day. The S Buses are express buses that operate on longer lines, with fewer stops. '''Christianshavn's Square''' is the major stop, with stops by 3 different buslines, and interchange to the Metro.
*'''2A''' starts at the Tingbjerg district in the [[Copenhagen/North-West|North Western]] part of Copenhagen, and runs through [[Copenhagen/Indre_By|downtown]], with stops at Rådhuspladen (the central square) and the central station, before it crosses the harbour, and runs through Christianshavn on Torvegade, with stops at Asiatisk Plads (Ministry of foreign affairs) and the Metro station on Christianshavn Square. It terminates at Kastrup metro station on [[Amager]]. *'''9A''' starts at Glostrup Station in the suburb of Glostrup, west of Copenhagen. Run through [[:Wikipedia:Rødovre|Rødovre]], [[:Wikipedia:Vanløse|Vanløse]] and [[:Wikipedia:Frederiksberg|Frederiksberg]] before reaching [[Copenhagen/Indre_By|inner city]] with stops on [[:Wikipedia:Copenhagen_Central_Station|Copenhagen Central Station]] and [[:Wikipedia:Christiansborg_Palace|Christiansborg]]. It then crosses [[:Wikipedia:Knippelsbro|Knippelsbro]] bridge, and stops at [[:Wikipedia:Christianshavns_Torv|Christianshavn Square]] before passing [[:Wikipedia:Freetown_Christiania|Christiania]] and ends at [[:Wikipedia:Refshaleøen,_Copenhagen|Refshaleøen]]. *'''350S''' begins its journey in the suburb of Herlev, north west of Copenhagen. And runs through the [[Copenhagen/Indre_By|inner city]] with stops on Nørreport station and Kongens Nytorv square, before it crosses Knippelsbro bridge, and stops at Christianshavn Square. It continues onwards to [[Amager]], and terminates in picturesque Dragør south of the Airport.
=== By metro === The M1 or M2 metro lines stop at Christianshavn in the center of the district at Christianshavn Square. Interchange between the S-train system and the metro is provided at Nørreport station.
=== By boat ===
The water buses (Bus 901 and 902) have to stops on Holmen ('''Holmen nord''' and '''Opera'''). The water buses only sail in the summer.
You can sail in your own boat to Lynetten Marina.
The [https://www.stromma.com/en-dk/copenhagen/sightseeing/sightseeing-by-boat/ Canal-Tour Waterbus] have several stops on Holmen.
== Get around == [[Image:Overgaden Oven Vandet 18-28.jpg|thumb|colourful houses along the main Canal]] === By bus ===
Bus line '''9A''' enters Christianshavn over the Knippels bridge, from the city centre, and turns north at Christianshavns square, along Prinsessegade - where it stops at Vor Frelsers church, and Christianias main entrance, from where it continues on the bridge across to ''Holmen'' and terminates at Refshaleøen.
=== By bike ===
Christianshavn's small, quiet streets are excellent for sightseeing on a bike, and the free ''citybike'' system covers the entire district, there are stalls at Christianshavn torv, Asiatisk Plads (near the Ministry of Foreign Affairs), Church of our Saviour, at the main entrance to Christiania, and the central square of Holmen.
==See== [[Image:Amagergade 01.jpg|thumb|Old houses along Amagergade]] Christianshavn's main attraction, is Christianshavn itself. It bears some resemblance to [[Amsterdam]], with its quiet streets branching out from the main canal, flanked by old houses, in bright colours, some dating back to 18th century - one of the photogenic ones is '''Amagergade''' on the right side of the main street, at the end nearest the dam to Amager. And of course the green oasis of the free town [[Copenhagen/Christiania|Christiania]], with its creative home build houses, covered in its own guide.
* '''[[Copenhagen/Christiania|Christiania]]''' {{see | name= | alt=Fristaden Christiania; or just 'Christiania' or 'Staden' | url=http://www.christiania.org | email= | address= | lat=55.673589 | long=12.599711 | directions=Enter from Prinsessegade | phone= | tollfree= | hours=Open 24 hours | price=Free | wikidata=Q202206 | content=Since its establishment in a former military area in 1971, Christiania has been a partially self-governed and semi-legal community of {{acre|85}}. It has more than 1,000 inhabitants and more than a million people visiting every year. Christiania has bars, cafes, restaurants and concert venues. Christinia is car-free with small paths and a lot of green and water areas. Staden has throughout the years been a subject for controversy. In the 2010s, the district has moved towards legalisation and normalisation, and Danish law is now applied in Christiania. In September 2016, residents tore down "pusher street" after a fatal shooting occurred during a drug bust. Later drug dealers came back but Pusher Street was finally destroyed in April 2024. }} * {{see | name=Dansk Arkitektur Center | alt=Danish Architecture Centre | url=http://www.dac.dk/en/front-page/ | email=dac@dac.dk | address=Strandgade 27B | lat=55.67588 | long=12.59104 | directions= | phone=+45 3257 1930 | tollfree= | hours=Daily 10:00-17:00, W until 21:00 | price=Adults {{DKK|60}}, students {{DKK|40}}, under 18 years free. Free admission on Wednesdays 17:00-21:00 | lastedit=2017-09-18 | content=Each year, 5-10 exhibitions on Danish and foreign architecture can be seen here. Conferences, lectures and guided tours are held in connection with exhibitions. }}
[[Image:CphLilleMolle.jpg|thumb|Lille Mølle]]
* {{see | name=Nordatlantens brygge | alt= | url=http://www.bryggen.dk/ | email=bryggen@bryggen.dk | address=Strandgade 91 | lat=55.67764 | long=12.59668 | directions= | phone=+45 32 83 37 00 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 10:00-17:00, Sa Su 12:00-17:00 | price={{DKK|40}} | content=Nordatlantens Brygge is a cultural house where you can experience culture and arts from the North Atlantic region. Housed in an old dock building, there are 3 exhibition halls with changing exhibitions, performances and lectures. }} * {{see | name=Overgaden, institut for samtidskunst | alt=Institute for contemporary art | url=http://www.overgaden.org | email=info@overgaden.org | address=Overgaden Neden Vandet 17 | lat=55.67248 | long=12.59018 | directions= | phone=+45 32 57 72 73 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu-Su 13:00-17:00, Th 13:00-20:00 | price=Free | content=Established in 1986 by a group of local artists, Overgaden has for more than two decades been among the leading non-profit exhibition spaces for contemporary art in Copenhagen. }} * {{see | name=Skibene på Holmen | alt=The Historic ships on Holmen | url=http://www.skibepaaholmen.dk | email= | address=Elefanten, Nyholm | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+45 33 11 60 37 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=1 July-15 August 11:00-17:00 | price=Combination ticket: {{DKK|70}} | content=A collection of 3 cold war warships; The large Frigate ''Peder Skram'', The last Danish submarine ''Sælen'' famed for her participation in the War on Iraq in 2003 and protection of Norwegian waters during the Cold War. Also on display is P 547 ''Sehested'', the last fast attack boat in the Royal Danish Navy. Apart of the summer opening, the ships are open during most school breaks, and for larger groups at any time of year. }} *{{see | name=Spejdermuseet | alt=The scouts museum | url=http://www.spejdermuseet.dk | email= | address=Arsenalvej 100 | lat=55.6752141 | long=12.6023368 | directions= | phone=+45 32 64 00 59 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=W 17:00-20:00, Th 14:00-17:00, Su 11:00-15:00 | price=Free | content=Housed in a 250-year-old arsenal building, this museum showcases the history of the Scouts movement in Denmark, back to its beginning in 1905, through models, old equipment, posters etc. }} [[File:Treschows Stiftelse (Christianshavn.JPG|thumbnail|right|Treschows Stiftelse]] * {{see | name=Treschows Stiftelse | alt= | url= | email= | address=Overgaden oven Vandet 76-82 | lat=55.67477 | long=12.59642 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=This listed building complex was erected in 1850s to provide housing for women in need by what was later to become the Treschow foundation, created by the Copenhagen attorney Frederik Treschow of the famous Treschow family. Finding your way to the buildings may be a bit of a challenge, but you will be rewarded by the serene atmosphere that remains there. }} *{{see | name=Christianshavns Mound | alt=Christianshavns Vold | url= | email= | address= | lat=55.66930 | long=12.58891 | directions=enter just after crossing to Amager over the bridge Langebro; or from Torvegade a bit after crossing the bridge Knippelsbro | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Open 24 hours | price=Free | content=Christianshavns Mound is part of Copenhagen's old defense system. It is now an excellent area for a walk surrounded by green and water, passing a number of historical buildings, cafes and restaurants. Christianshavns Mound was built by Christian IV in 1618-23 and was further developed in the following centuries. The defense purpose ceased towards the end of the 19th century and public access to the area was successively established in 1961. }} * {{see | name=Copenhagen Contemporary | alt=CC | url=https://copenhagencontemporary.org/en/ | email=contact@cphco.org | address=Refshalevej 173A, 1432 Copenhagen K | lat=55.693099 | long=12.613395 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | hours=F-W 11:00-18:00, Th 11:00-21:00, M Tu closed | price=kr 120, kr 95 for students, children up to 17 are free | lastedit=2024-03-28 | content=As one of the great contemporary museums of Copenhagen it gives space to large-scale modern installations. Can be well combined with a visit to the Reffen food court area next to it. }}

* {{do | name=Christianshavns bådudlejning of café | alt=Christianshavn boat rental and cafe | url=http://www.baadudlejningen.dk | email=ohoj@baadudlejningen.dk | address=Overgaden neden Vandet 29 | lat=55.67276 | long=12.59109 | directions= | phone=+45 32 96 53 53 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=80 kr/hour | content=Interesting mix of a café on a floating dock in the canal, that also rents out rowing boats for sailing in the canals of Christianshavn. Also sells picnic baskets for the boats. }} *{{do | name=Kajak ole |alt=| url=http://www.kajakole.dk | email= | address=Strandgade 50 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+45 40 50 40 06 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=from 245 kr |lastedit=2018-09-23| content=This outfit conducts guided kayaking tours around the canals of Christianshavn and Holmen. The owner is a nice friendly dude, that has been doing his tours for more than 10 years. the 3 standard tours ranges between 1½-3 hours, but you can also request special individual tours. Also arranges hilarious kayak water polo games. }} *{{do | name=The Operahouse | alt= | url=https://kglteater.dk/ | email= | address=Ekvipagemestervej 10 | lat=55.68218 | long=12.60010 | directions= | phone=+45 33 69 69 33 | tollfree= | hours=17:00-last show | price=Price varies | content=Opened in 2005 in a striking building overlooking the harbour, designed by world renowned architect Henning Larsen. Mainly hosts operas, but occasionally shows contemporary music concerts, and plays. For operas there are always 25 tickets reserved for selling on the day. }} * {{do | name=Vor Frelsers Kirke | alt=Church of our saviour | url=http://www.vorfrelserskirke.dk/ | email=info@vorfrelserskirke.dk | address=Sankt Annæ Gade 29 | lat=55.67294 | long=12.5943 | directions= | phone=+45 32 54 68 83 | tollfree= | hours=Church 11:00-15:30, tower 09:00-20:00 (closed in January) | price=Tower full price: 69 kr | lastedit=2026-02-18 | content=Climb the 400 steps to the top of the spire of the church for panoramic views of Copenhagen. The last steps are on the ''outside'' of the spire. Rumor has it that the designer threw himself out the tower, when he discovered the rotation of the stairs was the wrong way, so that sword fights would have to be fought with the left hand, however the spiraling stairs was meant to symbol the ascend into heaven. }}
==Buy== Shopping possibilities on Christianshavn aren't as extensive as in other parts of Copenhagen, but there several small and interesting stores dotted around Christianshavn's quite side streets, and on Christiania. Groceries can be bought in the Super Brugsen on the Christianshavns Torv, Christianshavns central square where the metro station is located.
*{{buy | name=Ceramic Studio and Shop | url=http://www.vincents.dk/ | email= | address=Wilders Plads 9B | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+45 32 96 29 20 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=This is a combined studio and shop for the two ceramicists Inge Vincent and Karin Michelsen, carrying products in the beautiful Scandinavian design tradition. }} *{{buy | name=Ginnungagab | url= | email= | address=Overgaden oven Vandet 4A | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+45 32 54 22 11 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 11:00-18:00, Sa 11:00-15:00 | price= | content=A place to go for some alternative souvenirs, the store is specialized in products of leather and wool, from Scandinavia and Greenland, like clothing, shoes and bags. It also carries the vikings preferred alcohol; mead, and organic vegetables. Have fun pronouncing the name, when asking directions for the store. }}
