==Connect==
{{IsPartOf|Copenhagen}} {{Geo|55.673611|12.593056}} {{guidedistrict}}

{{pagebanner|FrederiksbergHaveBanner.jpg|pgname=Frederiksberg}} [[File:Bentzonsvej & Langelands Plads, Frederiksberg.jpg|thumb|right|A typical street corner in Frederiksberg]] ''' Frederiksberg''' might look like a part of Copenhagen to a traveller, but the municipality of Frederiksberg is an enclave of 103,000 people (2022) inside the municipality of Copenhagen. The inhabitants of Frederiksberg have historically been richer than their neighbours around them, although the rest of Copenhagen is catching up. Frederiksberg still has more parks, big villas with gardens, wide avenues, etc.
==Understand==
Tourism information: [https://www.visitcopenhagen.com/frederiksberg Visit Copenhagen: Frederiksberg].
==Get in==
===By public transport=== The '''metro''' line crosses the northern part of Frederiksberg, so it is the best way to get there from [[Copenhagen Airport]], as well as several other areas of the city served by metro, such as [[Copenhagen/Amager|Amager]]. The following Metro stations are in Frederiksberg: * {{Marker|name=Forum|lat=55.6819 | long=12.5524}} * {{Marker|name=Frederiksberg|content=this undergroung station at the Falconer Allee is directly connected to the Frederiksberg Centret shopping centre|lat=55.6812 | long=12.5328 }} * {{Marker|name=Fasanvej|lat=55.6817 | long=12.5230}} * {{Marker|name=Lindevagn|lat=55.6835 | long=12.5129}} * {{Marker|name=Flintholm|content=a large interchange station for the metro, two S-Tog lines and many buses. Otherwise of little interest to tourists, as it is surrounded mostly by office and residential buildings and quite remote|lat=55.6857 | long=12.4989}}
From south to north, two lines of the '''S-Tog''' cross Frederiksberg - lines C (and its express variant H) closer to the centre and line F along the eastern border of Frederiksberg. The following stations on line C are in Frederiksberg: * {{Marker|name=Peter Bangs Vej|content=A station right over one of the main streets of Frederiksberg of the same name. You can change to the express bus 9A and some other buses here|lat=55.67805 | long=12.50362}} * '''Flintholm''' - interchange with metro and S-Tog line F, and the only stop in Frederiksberg where the express line H stops Line F has the following stops along the border of Frederiksberg: * {{Marker|name=KB Hallen|content=Next to the namesake complex of sports halls and tennis courts, as well as Peter Bangs Vej. Otherwise, mainly residential area|lat=55.67208 | long=12.49319}} * {{Marker|name=Altholm|content=Next to Roskildevej, the westwards extension of Vesterbrogade|lat=55.67772 | long=12.49233}} * '''Flintholm''' - interchange with metro and S-Tog lines C and H
===On foot=== The eastern part of Frederiksberg is so close to the Copenhagen Central Station that you may simply '''walk'''.
===By car=== Once you've made it to Copenhagen, Frederiksberg is as difficult or easy to reach by car as any other central district. If you're used to drive in European cities, Frederiksberg shouldn't be much of a problem for you.
[[File:Parking ticket machine in Frederiksberg.jpg|thumb|upright|Ticket machines are somewhat rare and inconspicuous.]]
Finding a '''parking''' spot can take a bit of time, but is by no means impossible. Note that Fredericksberg's parking rules are different from those in the rest of Copenhagen. Some information in English can be [https://www.frederiksberg.dk/en/english#parking-cd found on the official website]{{Dead link|date=February 2024 |bot=InternetArchiveBot }}. Most of Frederiksberg belongs to a single large parking zone (''Parkeringszone'' 2000) with consistent rules, but always watch out for signs imposing additional local restrictions. Within the zone, parking is free at night between 00:00 and 07:00, Saturday evenings from 17:00 on, and on Sundays and public holidays. Outside these hours, you get another 2 hours of free parking, after which you'll have to pay by the hour. Parking disks are ''not'' accepted, parking apps are preferred instead (there's a list of accepted apps [https://www.frederiksberg.dk/en/english#parking-cd on the website]{{Dead link|date=February 2024 |bot=InternetArchiveBot }}). You can also buy a digital ticket at one of the small green vending machines (no cash!). These are somewhat rare, though, and not very easy to spot if you don't know what you're looking for. You can find their locations on the parking information maps on the streets or on the [https://gis.frederiksberg.dk/kort/trafik-og-parkering.html online map] (activate map layer ''Trafik og mobilitet''). One advantage over at least some of the apps is that the machines also offer day tickets for 95 kr and week tickets for 475 kr.
As of 1 October 2023 a low emission zone has been introduced in Frederiksberg, see [[Driving in Denmark#Low Emission Zones]].

|55.6693,12.4936 |55.6758,12.4928}} Frederiksberg has a system of bus lines ([http://www.moviatrafik.dk/dinrejse/kort/trafikkort/Documents/Hovedstadsomraadet/BusserFrederiksberg.pdf map]{{Dead link|date=January 2023 |bot=InternetArchiveBot }}) with frequent stops that connect to and supplement the bus system of Copenhagen. The operator is the same and so is the ticketing system.
==See== [[Image:Copenhagen Zoo.jpg|thumb|Casual camel in the Copenhagen zoo, you can just make out the Zoo's landmark observation tower in the background]]
* {{see | name=City Hall (Rådhuset) | url=http://www.frederiksberg.dk/OmKommunen/English.aspx | email= | address=Smallegade 1-3 | lat=55.67863 | long=12.53142 | directions= | phone=+45 38 21 21 26 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tours the first Saturday each month at 13:00 | price= | content=The massive city hall took 10 years to complete, because the city council failed to see World War II coming, and war time turned out not to be the best period getting materials. The somewhat oversize building might be a result of rivalry with the city of Copenhagen, and its impressive city hall. Enjoy the great view from the tower 60 meters high, but call ahead to make an appointment }} * {{see | name=Storm P. Museum | url=http://www.stormp-museet.dk/ | email= | address=Frederiksberg Runddel | lat=55.67478 | long=12.53186 | directions=at the entrance to Frederiksberg Have | phone=+45 38 86 05 23 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Robert Storm Petersen (Storm P) was a famous Danish artist and author, his humorous works is often regarded as something quintessentially Danish. The museum owns more than 50,000 drawings (of which only some are on exhibition). Storm P is mostly known for his comedic drawings of "philosophical" vagabonds and his much-loved Storm P machines, that perform very simple tasks through an unnecessarily complex and usually humorous series of actions. }} * {{see | name=Cisternerne | alt=Museum of Modern Glass Art | url=http://www.cisternerne.dk | email= | address=Pile Alle 55 | lat=55.66804 | long=12.53056 | directions= | phone=+45 33 21 93 10 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Open F 14:00-18:00, Sa Su holidays 11:00-17:00. Closed Dec-Jan | price=70 kr | content=The museum of is the old, catacomb like, water cisterns under Søndermarken park opposite the main entrance to Zoo, and the unusual environs, combined with the stained glass art and various sculptures makes the museum quite unique. }} * {{see | name=Revymuseet | alt=The museum of revue theatre | url=http://www.revymuseet.dk/ | email=post@revymuseet.dk | address=Allégade 5 | lat=55.67540 | long=12.53169 | directions= | phone=+45 38102045 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu-Su 11:00-16:00 | price=35 kr | content=Revue theatre is still a very popular art form in Denmark, underpinned by the Danes love of black (or dry) humour. And since Frederiksberg has played an important role in the history of Danish theatre, it is only natural that you find a museum dedicated to art form here, telling its story from its beginning 200 years ago up to today's stand-up comedians. Unfortunately mainly caters to Scandinavians with some command of the local language, as the multimedia part of the museum, is the best. }} * {{see | name=Møstings Hus | alt= | url=http://www.frederiksberg.dk/moestingshus.aspx | email=kultur@frederiksberg.dk | address=Andebakkesti 5 | lat=55.67834 | long= 12.52776 | directions=at Smallegade | phone=+45 38 21 01 22 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu-F 11:00-16:00 | price= | content=Changing art exhibition in small but very cute house next to the Frederiksberg Garden and a small idyllic pond. It is open to any artist that applies and is accepted by the municipality. }} *{{see | name=The Bakkehus Museum | url=http://www.bakkehusmuseet.dk/ | email= | address=Rahbeks Allé 23 | lat=55.66844 | long=12.53205 | directions= | phone=+45 33 31 43 62 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu-Su 11:00-16:00 | price=30 kr | content= }} * {{see | name=Landbohøjskolens Have | alt=Horticultural Garden | url=https://plen.ku.dk/pfv/haven | email= | address=Bülowsvej 17 | lat=55.6796 | long=12.5421 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | hours=sunrise to sunset | price=free | lastedit=2023-08-03 | content=The University Garden of the University of Copenhagen's Frederiksberg campus is a relatively small but beautiful botanical garden with a focus on cultivated plants. }} * {{see | name=Music Museum | alt=Musikmuseet | url=http://en.natmus.dk/museums/the-danish-music-museum/ | email= | address=Rosenørns allé 22 | lat=55.68224 | long=12.55239 | directions= | phone=+45 4120 6313 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Sa Su 10:00-17:00 | price=Adult 50 kr | wikidata=Q12327962 | content=Trace the history of music, with a visit to Denmark’s most comprehensive collection of instruments from all over the world at the Danish Music Museum. }}
=== Frederiksberg Have === [[Image:Cph frederiksberghave.jpg|thumb|200px|The Chinese pavilion in Frederiksberg Have serves as a tea house on Sundays during the summer]] This park is one of Copenhagen's largest, most attractive and popular green spaces, covering more than 30 hectares (75 acres) of parkland. It used to be part of the palace grounds, and is a mix of baroque and romantic era landscape garden. When the weather is nice the park is full of life, with kids playing around, occasional soccer and Frisbee games, and young couples kissing on the benches. If you walk around to explore the park, you'll find a few surprises hidden around the park. The ''Apias Temple'', located on the hill leading up to the palace, is a Greek style temple build in 1802, the classic look combined with washed out graffiti, makes for some interesting photo opportunities, if you're a geek. Elsewhere the ''Chinese gazebo and bridge'' was constructed in 1803, and was used as a tea house for the royalty on the palace. It sits on an island surrounded by a canal, and is open for visitors every Sunday afternoon. For the kids and other playful souls there is also a ''park labyrinth'' facing Pile Allé, and in the winter there is an ice-skating rink at the main entrance (rentals available). And in the summer you can rent a boat and row around the canals. Consider ending your visit with lunch or dinner in the wonderful ''family gardens'' on the southern edge of the park.
* {{see | name=Frederiksberg Palace | url= | email= | address=Roskildevej 28 | lat=55.67139 | long=12.52511 | directions= | phone=+45 36 13 26 00 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tours the first Saturdays of each month at 11:00 and 13:00 | price= | content=The baroque palace dates back to 1699 and used to be a royal residence, today it functions as home of the Royal Danish Army Officers Academy. It can be viewed from the gardens or on the monthly tours. }} * {{see | name=Copenhagen Zoo | alt= | url=https://www.zoo.dk/en// | email= | address=Roskildevej 32 | lat=55.67169 | long=12.52327 | directions=Bus 4A, 6A or 26 | phone=+45 72 20 02 00 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Opening hours varies greatly throughout the season, but M-F 09:00-16:00 & Sa Su 09:00-18:00 are always safe bets. During high season daily: 09:00-21:00 | price= |lastedit=2018-09-23| content=The Copenhagen Zoo is one of the oldest in Europe tracing its history back to 1859. Its focus has shifted from displaying as many animals as possible, to providing large, world class facilities to the animals, earning the zoo a reputation as one of the best in the world. An open savannah area allows wildlife mixes with each other, and thete is a large elephant house designed by Norman Foster. It is the only zoo outside Australia to have Tasmanian devils on display. The zoo is amazingly kid-friendly, with many play areas, and some hands-on animal displays throughout the zoo. The zoo has a restaurant, fast-food places, and you are allowed to bring your own food, and eat in the picnic areas. }} * {{see | name=Svendsens Bådfart | alt=Rowing-boats | url= | email= | address= | lat= | long= | directions= | phone= +45 38 11 44 32 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=May-Sep: M-F 10:00-17:00, Sa Su 12:00-18:00 | price=30 kr | content=Tours take 20 minutes. }} *{{see | name=Haveselskabets Have | alt= | url=https://haveselskabet.dk/haveselskabets-have | email= | address=Pile Allé 6 | lat=55.67296 | long=12.53142 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | hours= | price=Free | lastedit=2023-08-03 | content=Several nice small gardens run by the Garden Society. }}
==Do==
* {{do | name=Forum | url=http://www.forumcopenhagen.dk/ | email=info@forumcopenhagen.dk | address=Julius Thomsens Plads 1 | lat=55.68109 | long=12.55310 | directions=at Forum Metrostation | phone=+45 32 47 20 00 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Big hall used for exhibitions, conferences and concerts. Their website is in English and worth checking out, as they often has international acts on the programme. }} * {{do | name=The Betty Nansen Theater | url=http://www.bettynansen.dk/ | email=billet@bettynansen.dk | address=Frederiksberg Allé 57 | lat=55.67405 | long=12.53700 | directions= | phone=+45 33 21 14 90 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Office: M-F 14:00-18:00, Sa 14:00-16:00 | price=300 kr | content=A good, popular and long-running local theatre, back from the heydays of Frederiksberg theatre, but English performances are rare, so it is best suited for Scandinavian speakers. }} * {{do | name=Camp X Theatre | url=http://www.campx.dk/?sc_lang=en | email=billet@campx.dk | address=Smallegade 2 & Frederiksberg Allé 102 | lat=55.67867 | long=12.53299 | directions=Next to City Hall | phone=+45 70 20 10 31 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Office: M-F 14:00-18:00, Sa 14:00-16:00 | price= | content=A modern, lightly experimental, theatre on Frederiksberg where English or other international performances, or visitors, are not unusual. }}
==Buy==
[[Image:CopenhagenPorcelainFactory.jpg|thumb|200px|porcelain factory]]
''Falkoner Allé'' one of Frederiksberg's main streets are well known for its many Computer stores, and is the place to go in Copenhagen if you need spare parts, a USB drive for your pictures or the like.
* {{buy | name=Can Family | alt= | url=http://www.yeswecancan.com/ | email=can@yeswecancan.com | address=Tullinsgade 5 | lat=55.67369 | long=12.55157 | directions= | phone=+45 31 79 63 47 | tollfree= | hours=M-F 12:30-17:30, Sa 11:00-15:00 | price= | lastedit=2023-08-08 | content=A cool combined shop, record label and gallery, featuring some nice and rather childlike paintings, records, toys and other odds and ends that the trio behind the shop happen to like. }} * {{buy | name=Frederiksberg Fleamarket | alt= | url=https://visitfrederiksberg.dk/loppetorv-paa-frederiksberg/ | email= | address= | lat=55.67806 | long=12.53281 | directions=Behind Frederiksberg City Hall | phone=+45 38 21 42 20 | tollfree= | hours=Apr to Oct: Sa 09:00-15:00 | price= | lastedit=2023-08-08 | content=The most popular of Copenhagen's flea markets, since the neighbourhood is rather affluent, you can often find good antiques and upscale secondhand fashion brands. Beers are often served from tents, and sometimes there is folksy live music. }} * {{buy | name=Frederiksberg Centeret | alt= | url=http://www.frbc-shopping.dk/ | email=frbc.info@dsc.dk | address=Falkoner Alle 21 | lat=55.68149 | long=12.53396 | directions=at Frederiksberg Metro station | phone=+45 38 16 03 40 | tollfree= | hours=M-F 10:00-19:00, Sa Su 10:00-17:00 | price= | lastedit=2023-08-08 | content=The only shopping mall in Frederiksberg, right by the metro station. 65 stores over 9000 m². Usual high street suspects, plus a few interesting stores. }} * {{buy | name=Royal Copenhagen Factory Outlet | alt= | url=https://www.royalcopenhagen.com/en-gb/find-us/outlets | email=factoryoutlet@royalcopenhagen.com | address=Søndre Fasanvej 9 | lat=55.67824 | long=12.52128 | directions= | phone=+45 38 34 10 04 | tollfree= | hours=M-F 10:00-18:00, Sa 10:00-15:00, Su 11:00-15:00 | price= | lastedit=2023-08-08 | content=This store is located on the grounds of what used to be the porcelain factory. The production has now moved elsewhere, but they still maintain this outlet store here, selling classical dinnerware, home decor and figurines. }}
* {{buy | name=Gourmand Ostespecialisten | alt= | url=https://frederiksbergost.dk/ | email=storgaard@pc.dk | address=Gammel Kongevej 155 | lat=55.67737 | long=12.537 | directions=about one block east of the City Hall | phone=+45 33 24 08 04 | tollfree= | hours=Tu-F 09:30-17:30, Sa 09:30-14:00 | price= | lastedit=2023-08-08 | content=Great cheese and other delicacies. Try their own "Sankt Thomas" cheese that is ripening in the cellars somewhere below Frederiksberg if you need a change from the regular Danish supermarket cheese. They also make great sandwiches around lunch time. }} === Værnedamsvej === {{infobox|A mouthwatering street|If you are overnighting it in an apartment, or staying in a hostel where a communal kitchen is available, or you'd like to blend in with the locals and picnic in one of the parks, consider dropping by some of Værnedamsvej's many food stores. It used to be known for its many butcheries, but they have all been replaced with a wide array of stores, modestly providing some of the city's best delicacies. On top of the stores listed here there are also an immigrant grocery store selling exotic food and fresh fruits and vegetables, and a store selling fresh fish. * '''Helges Ost''' — Speciality cheese store with a love for Italy. Beside the cheese, they also hand out freshly baked Danish and Italian bread, olives, sausages, Italian beer and other delicacies. * '''Juuls''' — One of the best specialized wine stores in the city with a good broad selection of wines from all over the world, but mainly European. It is also arguably Copenhagen's best place to buy Whiskey with over 350 different bottles to choose from. * '''Le Gourmand''' — A good francophile speciality food store, with many various food items from all over France, in almost every genre, from gourmandise to poisson. * '''Smagen af lykke''' — Made to order juices and smoothies, in every colour of the rainbow, some unusual combinations to be had, and even specially crafted recipes for diabetics etc. Also sells wraps. * '''Summerbird''' — A fantastic high end chocolate store, which despite its modest size, should make every girls mind wander. The Snowballs are expensive but fantastic, and the gift boxes are nice presents to bring home. }}
Værnedams is a small, but incredibly hip, street running between''Vesterbrogade'' and'' Gammel Kongevej''. It is mainly famous for, and colloquially known as, "The Food street" because of the solid number of speciality food stores and good take away stores located here. But endowed with its spot on location right on the border between the towns hippest district, [[Copenhagen/Vesterbro|Vesterbro]] and the most wealthy; Frederiksberg. Food is not the only thing on offer on the small street. If you are here, why not continue shopping in some of the cool fashion stores mixed in between the food stores, or buy you loved one some flowers from the impossibly cute floweriest right at the middle of the street. * {{buy | name=Karl Smart | url=http://www.karlsmart.com | email= | address=Værnedamsvej 11 | lat=55.67375 | long=12.54977 | directions= | phone=+45 33 25 28 33 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Th 10:00-18:00, F 10:00-19:00, Sa 10:00-16:00 | price= | content= }} * {{buy | name=Pigen & Diamanten | alt= | url=https://pigenogdiamanten.dk/ | email=mail@pogd.dk | address=Værnedamsvej 9 | lat=55.6736 | long=12.54978 | directions= | phone=+45 33 23 00 36 | tollfree= | hours=Tu-F 10:00-18:00, Sa 10:00-15:00 | price= | lastedit=2023-08-08 | content=The name means 'the Girl and the Diamond' in English, which should reveal that this is jewellery store. Exciting Danish designs and designers on sale, and a broad range which should soothe most tastes. }} * {{buy | name=Samsøe & Samsøe | url=http://samsoesamsoe.dk/ | email= | address=Værnedamsvej 12 | lat=55.67384 | long=12.55005 | directions= | phone=+45 33 22 16 38 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Th 10:00-18:00, F 10:00-19:00, Sa 10:00-16:00 | price= | content=Fashion for ladies and gents, Samsøe & Samsøe is one of the top Danish fashion brands, but this store also sells a few other brands like BZR, Acne, Nümph and Minimum. }} * {{buy | name=Tiger Lilly | url= | email= | address=Værnedamsvej 11 | lat=55.67377 | long=12.54975 | directions= | phone=+45 44 61 22 04 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Th 11:00-17:30, F 11:00-18:00, Sa 11:00-15:00, closed W and Su | price= | content=Funky second-hand fashion store for the girls, run by two local stylists. Boheme attitude, vintage dresses and new designer clothes resold for local hipsters in need of new outfits. }} * {{buy | name=Tres Bien | url= | email= | address=Værnedamsvej 4A | lat=55.67309 | long=12.55013 | directions= | phone=+45 33 22 23 83 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 10:00-18:00, Sa 10:00-15:00 | price= | content=Classic cut, but modern fashion for women }}
==Eat== === Budget === [[Image:M. G. Petersens Gamle Familiehave (2009).jpg|thumb|200px|One of the many cozy family gardens located along the Pile Allé.]] * {{eat | name=Cafe Sokkelund | url= | email= | address=Smallegade 36 | lat=55.679094 | long=12.526364 | directions= | phone=+45 38 10 64 00 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Th Sa 10:00-23:00, F 10:00-00:00, Su 10:00-21:00 | price= | content=Brasserie-style French cafe, whose 100-kroner brunch is a good deal, and the premier draw at this place. Anything else is fine and OK, but nothing you'll remember the minute you walk out of there. }} * {{eat | name=Mio | alt= | url=https://miopizza.dk | email= | address=Falkoner Alle 17 | lat=55.68072 | long=12.53378 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | hours=Su-Th 11:30-21:00, F Sa 11:30-21:30 | price=veg pizza 49 kr, meat 59 kr, dessert 21 kr | lastedit=2025-10-25 | content=Often packed in peak times, this tiny takeaway pizza joint probably offers the best value in Copenhagen with good medium-sized pizzas for a very low price. You can either take them to the neighbouring bar or enjoy them in the Frederiksberg Have park, not far away, on a sunny day. }}
=== Mid-range === * {{eat | name=Hansens Old Family Garden | alt= | url=http://www.hansenshave.dk/ | email=info@hansenshave.dk | address=Pile Alle 10-12 | lat=55.672779 | long=12.531414 | directions= | phone=+45 36309257 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=All year 11:00-00:00 | price=Menu is 350 kr, 140 kr for lunch | content=The oldest of the Frederiksberg garden restaurants. The main building is from 1630. Serves very traditional Danish food in mix between a Garden and an outdoor German style Beer hale, but also has indoor seating. No music played }} * {{eat | name=Falernum | url=http://www.falernum.dk | email= | address=Værnedamsvej 16 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+45 33 22 30 89 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-W 08:00-00:00, Th F 08:00-02:00, Sa 10:00-02:00, Su 10:00-23:00 | price= | content=A good place to buy breakfast or lunch as it rather unusually opens at 08:00, and you can either opt for eating at the long tables, or ask to take away - the freshly baked bread is yummy. In the evening it turns into a rather nice wine bar, with a good an extensive menu. }} * {{eat | name=Kartellet | url=http://kartellet.nu | email= | address=Allégade 8A | lat=55.675726 | long=12.533042 | directions= | phone=+45 33 22 36 42 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Th 10:30-22:00, F Sa 10:00-00:00, Su 10:00-21:00 | price=Mains from ~100 kr, sets from 172 kr | content=Located in one of the old houses along Allégade, with outdoor seating in small garden facing the street, and indoor seating in tasteful large room. A good brunch menu (130 kr), Café lunch (burgers, sandwiches, etc.) and a small, French-inspired menu in the evening, which is nothing extraordinary, but pretty good and at reasonable prices. }} * {{eat | name=Krøgers Family Garden | url=http://www.kroegershave.dk/ | email=info@kroegershave.dk | address=Pile Alle 18 | lat=55.671157 | long=12.530641 | directions= | phone=+45 36450897 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=only during summer (May-Sep) | price=lunch platter at 168 kr | content=Only dating back to 1864, this is the youngest of the family gardens, where you sit outside on long benches drinking beer while garbling in traditional Danish fare. Live music every weekend in the summer }} * {{eat | name=Bjørnekælderen | url=http://www.bjornekaelderen.dk/ | email= | address=Frederiksberg Alle 55 | lat=55.674011 | long=12.537396 | directions=next to the Betty Nansen theatre | phone=+45 33 31 62 52 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Sa 11:30-23:00, Su 11:30-15:00 | price=3-course dinner 425 kr with wine. Theatre menu 225 kr | content=A solid restaurant serving (slightly updated) Danish slow food made from the ground up, with fresh ingredients. And classic Smørrebrød (Ryebread sandwiches) for lunch. Small outdoor seating area and there is also seating in an indoor pavilion.
}} * {{eat | name=La Fiesta Mexicana | url=http://www.lafiestamexicana.dk/ | email= | address=Frederiksberg Alle 21 | lat=55.673295 | long=12.544957 | directions= | phone=+45 33 21 77 50 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Daily 17:00-01:00 | price=3 -course menu: 179-199 kr | content=Excellent Mexican food restaurant that lives up to American standards. Reservations strongly suggested. }}
===Splurge=== * {{eat | name=Formel B | url=http://www.formel-b.dk/ | email=info@formel-b.dk | address=Vesterbrogade 182 | lat=55.670652 | long=12.535775 | directions= | phone=+45 33 25 10 66 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Daily 18:00-01:00 (kitchen closes 22:00) | price=Set menu 850 kr, wine menu 850 kr | content=Top-notch French-inspired cuisine using classic, often forgotten, Danish ingredients. It is so tasty that it has earned this place a Michelin star. Staff is highly professional, but if you are not into that, it may all feel a bit impersonal. Reservation necessary. }} * {{eat | name=Mielcke & Hurtigkarl | alt= | url=http://www.mielcke-hurtigkarl.dk | email=info@mielcke-hurtigkarl.dk | address=Frederiksberg Runddel | lat=55.6741183 | long=12.5300367 | directions=In Haveselskabets Have | phone=+45 38348436 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Apr-Sep: W-Su lunch and dinner, Oct-Dec: Th-Sa dinner, Jan-Mar: closed | price=Menu 750 kr, wine menu 750 kr | content=Experimental gourmet restaurant by one of Denmark's finest chefs, you pay for it too, but it is usually worth it. }}
==Drink==
=== Bars ===
* {{Drink|name=Dupong|url=https://dupong.dk|address=Griffenfeldsgade 52|lat=55.68613231718516|long=12.552546844089324|hours=W 18:00-00:00, Th 18:00-01:00 F Sa 18:00-03:00 |price=35 kr for the bat rental (one drink included). Water free |lastedit=2025-10-25|content=Get some drinks and play round-the-table ping-pong or table-soccer all night long. When you had enough, turn in your bat for a free drink and head downstairs for a more traditional bar setup.}}
*{{drink | name=Café Intime | url=http://www.cafeintime.dk | email= | address=Allégade 25 | lat=55.677223 | long=12.532205 | directions= | phone=+45 38 34 19 58 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Daily 18:00-02:00 | price= | content=A really cosy, and as the name suggests; intimate bar, with candle light, coloured lamps and purposely messy decoration. Frequent live Jazz and Classic music acts, which also turns really intimate as there isn't a lot of space, after a piano has been set up. Traditionally a gay bar, but straight customers are neither turned away nor frowned upon. Very friendly staff! }} * {{drink | name=Cafe Kellerdirk | url=http://www.kellerdirk.dk/ | email= | address=Frederiksberg Allé 102 | lat=55.674984 | long=12.533184 | directions= | phone=+45 33 25 22 53 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Th 11:00-00:00, F Sa 11:00-05 | price=Cover charge on Weekends | content=Named after two of the biggest names in Danish theatre, this place has a history going back nearly 200 years, though the current buildings has been converted from a theatre build in 1919. Salsa night every Monday (and Salsa courses the first Monday of the month), it is also staged for stand-up comedy (Danish) and frequent live music. Weekends it turns into a nightclub (over-30 crowd). Serves lunch and brunch during the day. }} * {{drink | name=Cafe Svejk | url=http://www.cafesvejk.dk/ {{dead link|December 2020}} | email= | address=Smallegade 31 | lat=55.678892 | long=12.527471 | directions= | phone=+45 38 86 25 60 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu-Sa 12:00-02:00, Su M 12:00-00:00 | price= | content=Located at the northern entrance to Frederiksberg Have, this small bar serves Czech Bohemia Regent beer (its own import - the bar is named after a Czech cartoon). Sometimes there are football matches and sometimes there is live music, but that is all rather random. }} * {{drink | name=Guldfasanen | url=http://www.guldfasanen.dk/ | email= | address=Nordre Fasanvej 1 | lat=55.679554 | long=12.521283 | directions= | phone=+45 38 34 20 71 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Sa 11:00-02:00, Su 11:00-00:00 | price=Beers from 13 kr, 10 shots for 100 kr before 22:00 | content=A local watering hole, popular with students from the nearby CBS university, but also with local alcoholics who are mostly there for the cheap beer, while students prefer the basic but very reasonably priced cocktails and "kander" (a cocktail pitcher for 145-189 Kr). This place for heavy drinking and smoking. }} * {{drink | name=Vinstue 90 | alt= | url=http://www.vinstue90.dk {{dead link|August 2018|December 2020}} | email= | address=Gammel Kongevej 90 | lat=55.67514 | long=12.54769 | directions= | phone=+45 33 31 84 90 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Very traditional (and smoky) wine bar with wine, port wine, and '''slow beer''' which is poured in exactly 12½ minutes without the use of carbon dioxide. You are allowed to bring food from the nearby smørrebrød store. }}
=== Coffee ===
* {{drink | name=Granola | url=http://www.granola.dk | email= | address=Værnedamsvej 5 | lat=55.6734273 | long=12.549794 | directions= | phone=+45 33 25 00 80 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Daily 07:00-19:00 | price= | content=Go here to buy a good cup of coffee, in this cosy resurrected coffee shop, with thick dark wooden classic interior. }}
==Sleep== [[Image:Frederikberg city hall.jpg|thumb|Frederiksberg City hall at night]]
===Budget=== * {{sleep | name=Euroglobe | alt= | url=http://www.hoteleuroglobe.dk | email= | address=Niels Ebbesensvej 20 | lat=55.678226 | long=12.549624 | directions= | phone=+45 33 79 79 54 | tollfree= | fax= | checkin= | checkout= | price=Single room: 400 kr, 2-4 bed rooms 450-800 kr | content=A budget hotel, which seems more like a hostel with shared bathrooms, a TV room and a simple kitchenette on one of the floors. It is pretty outdated, but at least this is reflected in the prices. }} * {{sleep | name=CAB INN Copenhagen Express | alt= | url=http://www.cabinn.com/English/ | email= | address=Danasvej 32-34 | lat=55.679613 | long=12.550329 | directions= | phone=+45 33 21 04 00 | tollfree= | fax= | checkin= | checkout= | price=Rooms from 525 kr | content=This budget hotel is pretty well reviewed for what it is, but it could use a makeover. Has both standard rooms and family rooms with bunk beds. Free Wi-Fi in the lobby and in selected rooms. }} * {{sleep | name=CAB INN Scandinavia | alt= | url=http://www.cabinn.com/English/ | email= | address=Vodrofssvej 55 | lat=55.680803 | long=12.555522 | directions= | phone=+45 35 36 11 11 | tollfree= | fax= | checkin= | checkout= | price=Rooms from 525 kr | content=Quite large hotel with small rooms, which is to be expected from a Danish budget hotel. Many rooms have bunk beds. On the upside it is centrally located and very close to the Forum metro stop, and staff is usually welcoming and accommodating. }}
===Mid-range=== * {{sleep | name=Avenue Hotel | url=http://www.avenuehotel.dk | email=info@avenuehotel.dk | address=Åboulevard 29 | lat=55.684718 | long=12.552585 | directions= | phone=+45 35 37 31 33 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=Single 795 kr, double 995 kr. Family and weekend offers. | checkin= | checkout= | content=A well-located mid-range hotel, with an impressive lobby and common facilities for the price - including sofas and a fireplace. The rooms are quite big by Copenhagen standards, nicely furnished and clean, though the carpets could use a replacement as they are beginning to have small stains many places. The rooms facing the main street can have noise problems, if you want the windows open. Internet is pricey. }} * {{sleep | name=Hotel Josty | url=http://www.josty.dk | email= | address=Pile Alle 14A | lat=55.673768 | long=12.529874 | directions=near the entrance to Frederiksberg Have | phone=+45 38 86 90 90 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=Single 700 kr, double 900 kr, suites 1400 kr | checkin= | checkout= | content=It was founded in 1813 by the Italian artist Agostino Taddei. The restaurant is open for lunch but otherwise reserved for bigger parties. Josty operates a boutique hotel with 7 rooms of varying size, which are a bit worn, but if you can live with that they are individually furnished with lavish antique interiors and chandeliers - no Nordic minimalism here. The place used to be used by the king to keep mistresses when he lived in the nearby castle. Breakfast is not included, but is reasonable and cheap at 65 kr. }}
==Connect== * {{listing | name=Frederiksberg Bibliotek | alt=Library | url=http://fkb.dk | email= | address=Falkoner Plads 3 | lat=55.68075 | long=12.52994 | directions= | phone=+45 38 21 18 00 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Th 10:00-19:30, F 10:00-17:00, Sa 10:00-14:00 | price= | content=Free Internet }}
==Cope==
[[Image:FrederiksbergHospital.jpg|thumb|Frederiksberg Hospital]]
* {{do | name=Frederiksberg Svømmehal | url=http://www.frederiksbergsvoemmehal.dk/ | email= | address=Helgesvej 29 | lat=55.68436 | long=12.53206 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=34 kr | content=Go for a swim }}

{{IsPartOf|Copenhagen}}
{{guidedistrict}}

revision.comment: /* Sankt Hans Torv nightlife */ Updated listing for Ølbaren
revision.text:
{{pagebanner|Noorebrobanner.jpg}} [[File:Nørrebrogade - beginning.jpg|thumb|350px|A view across the Sortedams Sø lake into Nørrebro.]] '''Nørrebro''' is an inner district of [[Copenhagen]] and perhaps the most multicultural part of the city. A prime cross-section of contemporary Danish society is on offer here, ranging from boutique shopping to grand middle-class areas through to areas revitalised by immigration.
==Understand ==
The many Middle Eastern immigrants in the area around Nørrebrogade, the district's main road artery, have led to the area being called '''Little Arabia'''. This epithet is more racist than celebratory - regardless of this, the area makes for a lively excursion. There are many clothing, jewelry and grocery stores carrying Arabian signs and products - not to forget the multitude of cheap shawarma places offering a cheap bite of Middle Eastern cuisine. The Muslim attitude to alcohol aside, the multiculturalism is very visible, in that the nightlife here is very vibrant - especially the southern part of the district near the Lakes, around Sankt Hans Torv.
The northwestern part of the district across the S-train tracks, sometimes known as Bispebjerg, but more often referred to as Nord-Vest (North West), is one of — probably even the — last bastions of working class Copenhageners. Don't expect much in the way of sights, fancy cafes, stores or anything like that, but if nothing else, it offers a peak into some of the not-so-rich and not-so-equal lives of those living in one of the richest and most equal societies in the world. Two of the '''cheapest accommodation''' options of Copenhagen are here.
==Get in== {{Mapframe|55.69986|12.52803|width=500|zoom=13|staticmap=Norrebro map.png}}
{{Mapmask |55.6817,12.5585 |55.6826,12.5592 |55.6873,12.5642 |55.6914,12.5704 |55.6963,12.5615 |55.6981,12.5567 |55.6987,12.5552 |55.6992,12.5543 |55.7032,12.5589 |55.7036,12.5578 |55.7088,12.5559 |55.7102,12.5550 |55.7114,12.5533 |55.7118,12.5524 |55.7116,12.5505 |55.7118,12.5485 |55.7126,12.5468 |55.7135,12.5459 |55.7152,12.5452 |55.7197,12.5419 |55.7194,12.5409 |55.7190,12.5404 |55.7167,12.5385 |55.7156,12.5370 |55.7140,12.5355 |55.7134,12.5351 |55.7144,12.5342 |55.7153,12.5330 |55.7159,12.5320 |55.7163,12.5311 |55.7181,12.5314 |55.7189,12.5281 |55.7182,12.5254 |55.7174,12.5228 |55.7168,12.5215 |55.7168,12.5186 |55.7172,12.5159 |55.7176,12.5146 |55.7177,12.5137 |55.7177,12.5129 |55.7174,12.5116 |55.7168,12.5111 |55.7152,12.5106 |55.7148,12.5097 |55.7147,12.5078 |55.7144,12.5066 |55.7139,12.5059 |55.7131,12.5054 |55.7126,12.5044 |55.7124,12.5037 |55.7117,12.5033 |55.7115,12.5027 |55.7115,12.4995 |55.7116,12.4988 |55.7122,12.4977 |55.7125,12.4966 |55.7125,12.4957 |55.7121,12.4948 |55.7115,12.4944 |55.7113,12.4944 |55.7110,12.4938 |55.7109,12.4928 |55.7111,12.4917 |55.7110,12.4903 |55.7108,12.4894 |55.7104,12.4890 |55.7091,12.4882 |55.7089,12.4877 |55.7087,12.4868 |55.7087,12.4859 |55.7083,12.4858 |55.7078,12.4850 |55.7060,12.4899 |55.7056,12.4914 |55.7051,12.4945 |55.7048,12.4970 |55.7044,12.4992 |55.7065,12.5142 |55.7023,12.5121 |55.6971,12.5064 |55.6946,12.5100 |55.6951,12.5110 |55.6960,12.5155 |55.6968,12.5257 |55.6961,12.5266 |55.6956,12.5275 |55.6957,12.5288 |55.6958,12.5303 |55.6962,12.5301 |55.6967,12.5306 |55.6967,12.5323 |55.6967,12.5322 |55.6974,12.5320 |55.6978,12.5330 |55.6977,12.5334 |55.6976,12.5340 |55.6975,12.5358 |55.6975,12.5365 |55.6976,12.5373 |55.6977,12.5383 |55.6968,12.5382 |55.6967,12.5367 |55.6965,12.5367 |55.6963,12.5367 |55.6962,12.5366 |55.6961,12.5365 |55.6960,12.5361 |55.6958,12.5359 |55.6957,12.5358 |55.6952,12.5356 |55.6950,12.5348 |55.6949,12.5350 |55.6944,12.5355 |55.6940,12.5357 |55.6937,12.5358 |55.6934,12.5359 |55.6930,12.5359 |55.6927,12.5359 |55.6924,12.5359 |55.6921,12.5360 |55.6917,12.5362 |55.6912,12.5365 |55.6910,12.5368 |55.6909,12.5370 |55.6906,12.5376 |55.6893,12.5397 |55.6890,12.5401 |55.6888,12.5403 |55.6884,12.5406 |55.6880,12.5410 |55.6877,12.5415 |55.6874,12.5420 |55.6870,12.5430 |55.6866,12.5444 |55.6865,12.5447 |55.6855,12.5464 |55.6854,12.5469 |55.6853,12.5473 |55.6852,12.5487 |55.6850,12.5515 |55.6850,12.5528 |55.6849,12.5533 |55.6848,12.5538 |55.6845,12.5545 |55.6843,12.5550 |55.6841,12.5553 |55.6837,12.5555 |55.6836,12.5553 |55.6835,12.5551 |55.6817,12.5539 |55.6816,12.5542 |55.6816,12.5548 |55.6818,12.5574 |55.6817,12.5585}}
=== By train === Metro line M3 connects the district with the rest of the city. The S-train system's ring '''line F''' runs along the northern border of the district, with two stops '''Bispebjerg''' which is not really interesting attraction wise, and the district's namesake station '''Nørrebro''' which lies at the northern end of Nørrebrogade. The most common option is to take the train or Metro to '''Nørreport''' station, and walk from there, or catch one of the frequent buses running along Nørrebrogade.
=== By bus === *'''3A''' sets off at Nordhavn Station on [[Copenhagen/Østerbro|Østerbro]] and enter Nørrebro on Tagensvej with stops at Sankt Hans Torv, Nørrebrogade and Stengade. From there it continues across to [[Copenhagen/Frederiksberg|Frederiksberg]], doing a loop around the center, before terminating in Sydhavnen. *'''4A''': Also does a half-loop around the city centre, serving the northern part of Nørrebro with several stops, from its northern terminus at Svannemøllen station. The most important stops being Nørrebro station and Lundtoftegade, before it too crosses Åboulevarden and enters [[Copenhagen/Frederiksberg|Frederiksberg]]. It continues its loop all the way to [[Amager]] *'''5A''': If your accommodation is on Nørrebro, this line is the best option, as it sets out from the Airport, and runs through the [[Copenhagen/Indre By|Inner City]], stopping at Nørreport station for interchange with the Metro and S-Train lines. It continues through the length of Nørrebrogade, with stops at Elmegade and Ravnsborggade (for shopping), Nørrebro's runddel square, Nørrebro Station (For interchange with S-train line F, and Bellahøj (for the Camping and Youth hostel accommodation). It terminates at Husum torv in the northern suburbs. *'''6A''' has very little use for tourists, it runs between Rødovre and Emdrup through the inner city, with stops at Tagensvej and Bispebjerg station on Nørrebro. *'''350S''' is an express line running between Ballerup in the northern suburbs, and the lovely village of [[Copenhagen/Amager|Dragør]] on Amager. It is one of the two buslines serving the length of Nørrebrogade, with the same major stops as line 5A
=== By foot === Nørrebro lies a within a manageable walking distance from Nørreport station, and the northern entry point to Strøget. Walk in North-eastern direction down Frederiksborggade, until you reach the bridge across the lakes, once you've passed it, you're on Nørrebro.
== Get around == Most of the interesting places on Nørrebro are concentrated southern part of the district, nearest to the city center, so it is easy to get around to the various sights on foot, or even better, on bike as the locals do. Bus lines 5A and 350S runs the length of Nørrebrogade, the major artery, if your feet gives up on you.
==See== [[Image:Søren Kierkegaard grave 2005.jpg|thumb|Søren Kierkegaard grave]]
* {{see | name=Assistens Cemetery | alt=between Nørrebrogade, Jagtvej and Kaptelvej. | url=http://www.assistens.dk/ | email= | address=Kapelvej 4 | lat=55.6895 | long=12.5530 | directions= | phone=+45 35 37 19 17 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=08:00-20:00 | price=Free | content=Both a cemetery and a peaceful park, replete with sunbathers and joggers. The A-section has big trees, bicycle paths and historical graves, including such famous Danes as the philosopher Kierkegaard, children's writer Hans-Christian Andersen and Scherfig. The place has very special atmosphere, in that it also doubles as a park, so you can see people having picnic or tanning in the sun, between the often impressive tombstones. }} * {{see | name=Barbie Doll Museum | url=http://www.barbiemuseum.dk/ | email= | address=Borups Allé 180 | lat=55.697155 | long=12.525411 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 10:00-15:00, Sa Su 10:00-14:00 | price=25 kr | content=Insanely large, private collection of Barbie dolls from 1959 onwards. The museum is only open by appointment with the owner, Lene Darlie Pedersen, who will also act as a guide. For appointment, call +45 38 10 30 23. }}
[[Image:Grundtvigskirken.jpg|thumb|Grundtvigskirken]]
* {{see | name=Grundtvigskirken | url=http://www.grundtvigskirke.dk/ | email= | address=På Bjerget 14 B | lat=55.716317 | long=12.534562 | directions= | phone=+45 35 81 54 42 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Sa 09:00-16:00, Su 12:00-16:00 | price=Free | content=Is a huge brickwork church at Bispebjerg, designed by Peder Klint and finished by his son, famous architect Kaare Klint. It is a peculiar mix of a traditional Danish village church, and bombastic Gothic style. }} *{{see | name=Herman Stilling Museum | url=http://www.hermanstillingmuseet.dk/ | email= | address=Kapelvej 2 | lat=55.689874 | long=12.553721 | directions= | phone=+45 33 12 18 69 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Sa Su 14:00-17:00 | price=20 kr | content=Herman Stilling was a Danish author and painter (1925-1996), who lived most of his life on Nørrebro. In 2003 a small museum was opened in the old stables by Assistentens cemetery, with many of his paintings of trolls and gnomes. }} *{{see | name=Politimuseet | alt=Police Museum | url=https://politi.dk/politimuseet | email=politimuseum@politimuseum.dk | address=Fælledvej 20 | lat=55.69005 | long=12.56059 | directions= | phone=+45 40 32 58 88 | tollfree= | hours=Tu Th Sa Su 11:00-16:00 | price=Adult 50 kr, child free | lastedit=2021-01-11 | content=Housed next to a prison, in an old police station. This museum has exhibitions portraying both cunning criminal cases, and witty police work, including artifacts from the cases displayed. }} *{{see | name=VerdensKulturCenteret | alt=The World cultural center | url=https://vkc.kk.dk | email= | address=Nørre Allé 7 | lat=55.69158 | long=12.56082 | directions= | phone=+45 23 29 79 06 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 08:00-22:00 | price=Free | lastedit=2017-11-24 | content=Run by the municipal administration, this is house is fittingly located in the most ethnic diverse district of Copenhagen. Houses festivals, lectures, art exhibitions, concerts and film screenings, organized by various ethnic minority associations in the city. Also has a cheap cafe, with a single daily dish of the day (lunch: 45 kr and dinner: 65 kr). }}
==Do== [[Image:Norrebro1.jpg|thumb|Station is a local cultural stage and exhibition center]] [[Image:UtterslevMose.jpg|thumb|Utterslev Mose]]
*{{do | name=Empire Bio | url=http://www.empirebio.dk/ | email= | address=Guldbergsgade 29 F | lat=55.6918626 | long=12.5557097 | directions= | phone=+45 35 36 00 36 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=12:00-last show | price=from 50 kr | content=Independent Cinema, which is often described as the best in Copenhagen, despite - or because of - its modest size. Balances between independent productions and mainstream movies. As is the case with all Danish cinemas, international movies are shown in their original language. }} *{{do | name=Folkets Hus | alt=House of the People | url=http://www.folketshus.dk/ | email= | address=Stengade 50 | lat=55.68724 | long=12.55435 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Along with the park in front of the house, the place is a product of the squatting movement from the 1980s, and still carries some of the spirit of the old days. Go here for a cup of coffee, discussions, lectures, concerts or parties with left-wing peers. Or simply ooze or have a barbecue in the nice park in front of the house. }} *{{do | name=Grøndal Centeret | url=http://www.sport.kk.dk/groendalcentret | email= | address=Hvidkildevej 64 | lat=55.6998339 | long=12.5153538 | directions= | phone=+45 38 34 11 09 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=from 06:45-00:00 | price=Varies | content=Grøndalscentret is a huge indoor sports complex, one of the largest in Northern Europe. Go for a good game of squash or bowling, do some swings in the gulf simulator or at the fencing operator, or a work out in the fitness center. And wind down in the cafe after wards. It is quite close to the Youth Hostel and Camping grounds. }} *{{do | name=Kaleidoskop - K1 | url=http://www.kaleidoskop.dk/ | email= | address=Nørrebrogade 37 | lat=55.688659 | long=12.558582 | directions= | phone=+45 70 26 53 02 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Ticket sales: 12:00-16:00 | price=30-165 kr | content=Kaleidoskop is stage for modern and experimenting theater performances in the heart of Nørrebro, and has been running for 15 years. }} *{{do | name=Literaturhaus | alt=The house of literature | url=http://www.literaturhaus.dk/ | email= | address=Møllegade 7 | lat=55.69067322 | long=12.55572080 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Housed in a former church, this is Denmark’s first live venue for literature. Here audiences can experience readings by national and international writers, participate in discussions, or participate in events cent red on music, film and other art forms or on their cross-fertilisation with literature. }} *{{do | name=Nørrebro Theater | url=http://www.nbt.dk/ | email= | address=Ravnsborggade 3 | lat=55.68787921 | long=12.56119251 | directions= | phone=+45 35 200 900 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Ticket sales: 06:00-18:00 | price=150-350 kr | content=Theatre on Nørrebro, with a focus on being the peoples theater, with down to earth plays, often edging over into Comedy. Mostly spoken, Danish performances, so probably best suited for Scandinavians. }} *{{do | name=Lygten Station | url=http://lygtenstation.dk/ | email= | address=Lygten 2A | lat=55.70137 | long=12.53747 | directions= | phone=+45 33 55 66 77 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Opening hours varies | price=Usually free | content=Cultural house and concert venue in a beautiful old disused station building, right by Nørrebro station. The area outdoors has tables and serves beer in the summer, inside there is a stage. Hosts art exhibitions, concerts (jazz and rock), film screenings etc. according to the program posted on their website. }} *{{do | name=Ungdomshuset Dortheavej | alt= | url=http://ungdomshuset.dk/english-info.aspx | email= | address=Dortheavej 61 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=When authorities decided to clear out ''Ungdomshuset'' (Jagtvej 69) on Nørrebro, it ended up in an Orwellian nightmare, with widespread riots that trashed the whole neighborhood. The movement behind the old house, is rebuilding and relocating to Nord-Vest. The house is home for the far left, anarchist, and punk movements of Copenhagen. Hosts concerts, parties, open kitchens, Yoya classes etc. Also has a bar, workshops, etc. }} * Go for a walk in '''Utterslev Mose''', 2 km² of lakes, grass, bicycle lanes, walking paths, and streams.
==Buy== Ravnsborggade and Elmegade are two side streets to Nørrebro's main artery, Nørrebrograde, and they are packed with small, interesting stores. Start here at lunch, and grab a bite in one of the many small places dotted around the neighborhood, offering cheap, quality food. Do your shopping in some of the city's most unique stores, and finish the day of with a celebratory drink, on - or around - Sankt Hans Torv, with its excellent nightlife options (check the drink listings below). Just don't get so carried away that you leave your shopping bags behind. Amazingly, it is all within a 500-m radius.
===Ravnsborggade=== Is the 2nd street on your right, after you cross the lakes from the downtown area. It is mostly famous for its large number of antique stores, covering every price range, and which expand their stores out on the sidewalk in the summer months. It also has a fair amount of small and interesting fashion stores to breeze through - many of these are the fashion designers' own stores. The streets commercial association runs its [http://www.ravnsborggade.dk own website]{{Dead link|date=January 2023 |bot=InternetArchiveBot }}, and also arranges flea markets several times a year (dates are posted on the website). Please note that only stores with their own websites are listed here, in order of street numbers - there are many more options to choose from, including on the side streets where Antik Hallen (The Antique hall) on Sortedams Dossering by the lakes deserves a special mention.
[[Image:Ravnsborggade.jpg|thumb|One of the many antique stores on Ravnsborggade]]
* {{buy | name=66°North | alt=Fashion | url=https://www.66north.com/stores/stores-in-denmark/ | email= | address= | lat= | long= | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu-Th 11:00-17:30, F 11:00-18:00, Sa 10:00-14:00 | price= | content=This store run by an Icelandic company, sells high performance adventure clothing, mainly for cold conditions, and has both a men's and a ladies section. }} * {{buy | name=Strand Galleriet | alt=Gallery | url=http://www.strand-galleriet.dk | email= | address=Ravnsborggade 5 | lat=55.68831 | long=12.56148 | directions= | phone=+45 20 55 58 06 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Th-Sa 11:00-18, Sa 11:00-15:00 | price= | content=A nice little gallery run by a couple from Western Jutland, hence the rather odd name (The Beach Gallery). Nice attitudes, and friendly staff - lets locals borrow paintings home, and see if it fits with their home. }} * {{buy | name=Antikviteter & Kuriosa | alt=Antiques | url=http://www.kuriosa.dk/ | email= | address=Ravnsborggade 10 | lat=55.68888 | long=12.56238 | directions= | phone=+45 35 36 62 56 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Th 10:00-15:00, F 10:00-18:00, Sa 10:00-14:00 | price= | content=With 45 years in the business, this guy seems to be knowing what he is doing, and his specialty is brass. }} * {{buy | name=Veirhanen | alt=Antiques | url=http://veirhanen.dk | email= | address=Ravnsborggade 12 | lat=55.68846 | long=12.56213 | directions= | phone=+45 35 35 31 42 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=This store has a large catalogue and a well-developed website - with most of the items for sale posted online. }} * {{buy | name=Kim Antons & Co Antique | url=http://www.antons.dk | email= | address=Ravnsborggade 14A | lat=55.68863 | long=12.56225 | directions= | phone=+45 35 37 06 24 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 09:30-17:30, Sa 11:00-14:00 | price= | content=One of the more exclusive stores, dealing primarily with furniture from the 19th century, and prices mostly going from 5000 kr and up (way up) }} * {{buy | name=ABC Antiques | url=http://www.abcantik.dk {{dead link|August 2020|December 2020}} | email= | address=Ravnsborggade 15 | lat=55.68888 | long=12.56238 | directions= | phone=+45 35 37 01 58 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 11:00-17:30, Sa 11:00-14:00 | price= | content=This is one of the more cozy stores on the street, with wide variety from furniture to decor, and pieces going all the way between 18th century and modern classics time wise. }} * {{buy | name=Bungalow | alt=Textiles | url=http://www.bungalow.dk | email= | address=Ravnsborggade 17 | lat=55.68910 | long=12.56241 | directions= | phone=+45 35 85 40 65 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu-F 11:00-17:00, Sa 11:00-14:00 | price= | content=Bungalow has a large collection of home decor and textiles imported directly from India by the owner, but also sells other textiles in a wide variety of patterns and colors. }} * {{buy | name=Stig P | alt=Fashion | url=http://stigp.net/en/ | email= | address=Ravnsborggade 18 | lat=55.68917 | long=12.56286 | directions= | phone=+45 35 35 75 00 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Th 11:00-18:00, F 11:00-19:00, Sa 10:00-16:00 | price= | content=Fashion store for women, carrying Stig P's own brand, and several other international brands. As a twist, in spirit with the street, this store also sells antique kimono from Japan. }} * {{buy | name=Ingerslev Antiques | url=http://www.ingerslevantik.dk | email= | address=Ravnsborggade 22 | lat=55.68959 | long=12.56337 | directions= | phone=+45 35 37 88 89 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 11:30-17:30, Sa 11:30-15:00 | price= | content=A cozy antique store, where the dust in the corners and the general mess, are part of the concept - and you can choose whether to have a chat with the owner, or just breeze the store without feeling obliged to buy anything. }} * {{buy | name=Birte Magnussen Antiques | url=http://www.birtemagnussen.dk | email= | address=Ravnsborggade 22 | lat=55.68959 | long=12.56337 | directions= | phone=+45 35 24 16 30 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content= }}
===Elmegade=== Two streets further up Nørrebrogade on the same side as Ravnsborggade is Elmegade. This small street has many small and interesting fashion stores, and many options for quality take away, or a quick bite of sushi, organic bread, bagels or good sandwiches. Again only shops with a web presence are listed here.
[[Image:Shop signs on Elmegade, Copenhagen .jpg|thumb|Store signs on Elmegade]]
* {{buy | name=Fünf | alt= | url=http://www.funf.dk | email= | address=Elmegade 2 | lat=55.68937 | long=12.558 | directions= | phone=+45 35 37 13 80 | tollfree= | hours=M-F 11:00-18:00, Sa 11:00-16:00 | price= | content=Five young designers, each running their own brand, is behind this collective on Elmegade. Mainly women's fashion and accessories, but also a few items for the men around, mainly by designer Tolsing. }} * {{buy | name=Goggle | url=http://www.goggle-theshop.com | email= | address=Elmegade 3 | lat=55.68943 | long=12.55771 | directions= | phone=+45 35 37 50 49 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Th 11:00-18:00, F 11:00-19:00, Sa 11:00-16:00 | price= | content=It was one of the first fashion stores to open on the street, run by a Frenchman and an Aussie, who has built an almost fanatical following. Both for men and women, and from the affordable to the exclusive. }} * {{buy | name=Carhartt | url=http://www.carhartt-store.dk/ | email= | address=Elmegade 13 | lat=55.68974 | long=12.55811 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Th 11:00-18:00, F 11:00-19:00, Sa 10:00-16:00 | price= | content=A nice store specialized in the Detroit brand Carhartt (and Burton) producing men's apparel. It blends in nicely with the surrounding stores, which mainly offers Danish fashion. }} * {{buy | name=Hooha | url=http://www.hooha.dk | email= | address=Elmegade 14 | lat=55.68984 | long=12.55885 | directions= | phone=+45 35 37 60 37 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Th 11:00-18:00, F 11:00-19:00, Sa 11:00-15:00 | price= | content=Nice little store, with cool wall paintings, and a friendly staff. Runs their own brand where 10 freelance designers does the prints, but also sells a few other hard-to-get brands. }} * {{buy | name=Elektrocute | url=http://www.elektrocute.com {{dead link|August 2020|December 2020}} | email= | address=Elmegade 20 | lat=55.69002 | long=12.55939 | directions= | phone=+45 35 36 34 41 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=As the fantastic store name could hint, this underground shop sells clothing connected to the Electronica scene. But you can also browse the internet at the computer in the back of the store, buy one of the carefully selected compilation CDs, and even get a hair cut. }}
===Jægersborggade=== Likes to brand itself as the Berliner Quarter of Copenhagen. The small cobbled street has a good variety of speciality shops ranging from small food and coffee joints to ceramics to vintage clothes, and a few galleries to boot. Usually, the shops are open late (21:00 or later) every first Thursday of the month. Coming from central Nørrebro (by the Lakes), take a stroll through Assistens Cemetery and you'll find Jægersborggade as an extension of this path just across Jagtvej. The street has a [http://www.jaegersborggade.dk website] {{dead link|December 2020}}, but it's in Danish only.
* {{buy | name=Ruben & Bobby | url=http://www.rubenogbobby.dk/ | email= | address=Jægersborggade 6 | lat=55.6919654 | long=12.5451809 | directions= | phone=+45 36 96 64 31 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 10:00-14:00, W F 10:00-17:30, Th 10:00-20:00 | price= | content=A curious mix of second hand stuff like games and action figures, fashion, their own designs - mainly inspired from the 90ties. directions= | phone=+45 36 96 68 50 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=W F 10:00-16:00, Th 10:00-18:00, Sa 11:00-13::00 | price= | content=Award winning ceramics }} * {{buy | name=Karamelleriet |
