phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Sells cactuses of all sizes, from tiny succulents (from 35kr) to huge things. }}
==Eat== {{Eatpricerange|99 kr or less|100–250 kr|250 kr or more }}
The dining on Nørrebro is dominated by the ethnic diversity of the district. From the dozen of take-away stores dotted along Nørrebrogade and Elmegade, offering everything from Middle Eastern over Thai to Japanese cuisine. The restaurants are just as diverse, and the prices are among the cheapest in town.
===Budget=== '''Nørrebrogade''' is the schawarma street of Copenhagen with a large number of small restaurants competing on especially schawarmas. They start at 15 kr for a small schawarma in a pita and 20 kr for a large one in a home-made wrap.
[[Image:CphBlaagaard.jpg|thumb|Blågårds Plads]]
* {{eat | name=Ayuttaya | url=http://www.ayuttaya.dk | email= | address=Griffenfeldsgade 39 | lat=55.68600 | long=12.55281 | directions= | phone=+45 35 37 38 68 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu-Sa 12:00-23:00, Su 17:00-23:00 | price=Mains from 70 kr, menus at 135 kr | content=No frills Thai food restaurant, serving decent courses in a setting you would expect at this price level, wax table cloth, and tacky Thai decor. }} * {{eat | name=Kates Joint | url= | email= | address=Blågårdsgade 12 | lat=55.687009 | long=12.5587027 | directions= | phone=+45 35 37 44 96 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=17:30-22:30 | price=Cheapest dish is 85 kr | content=No, you don't get high here, but it is a cozy and intimate place, and a long time favorite of the locals, for its personality and good size portions of food from every corner of the planet. }} *{{eat | name=Lá Lúa | url=http://www.lalua.dk/ | email= | address=Frederikssundsvej 32 | lat=55.70222 | long=12.53274 | directions= | phone=+45 38 19 63 63 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=16:00-23:00 | price=Mains from 90 kr | content=Cheap Vietnamese restaurant, surprisingly good food considering the location and the price, but the environs are definitely non pretentious. }} * {{eat | name=Quattro Fontane | alt=italiensk restaurant | url=http://www.quattrofontane.dk | email=info@quattrofontane.dk | address=Guldbergsgade 3 | lat=55.69077 | long=12.55975 | directions= | phone=+45 35 39 39 31 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=16:00-00:00 | price=Cheapest main dish go for 64 kr | content=Decent Italian cuisine, cooked up by real Italians - which definitely isn't the norm in this price range. }}
===Mid-range=== * {{eat | name=Bodega | alt= | url=http://www.bodega.dk | email= | address=Kapelvej 1 | lat=55.69031 | long=12.55438 | directions= | phone=+45 35 39 07 07 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu-Th Sa 10:00-midnight, F Sa Sa 10:00-16:00, Su Sa 10:00-18:00 | price=Mains from 169 kr | content=A nice brasserie with cool interiors, and bar with live DJ on the weekends, great food (with a menu of the day Tuesdays and Thursdays at 115 kr) and good drinks - and beers from a micro brewery. }} * {{eat | name=Cafe 22 | alt= | url=http://www.cafe22.dk/ | email=info@cafe22.dk | address=Sortedam Dossering 21 | lat=55.68905 | long=12.56465 | directions= | phone=+45 35373827 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-W 09:00-00:00, Th-Sa 09:00-02:00, Su 09:00-00:00 | price=3 course menu 229 kr | content=Nice restaurant and separate cafe at the lakes. }} * {{eat | name=Mama Africa | alt= | url=http://www.mamaafrica.dk | email= | address=Griffenfeldsgade 17 | lat=55.68818 | long=12.55459 | directions= | phone=+45 35 39 49 89 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=18:00-22:00 | price=Mains from 168 kr | content=One of the only sub-Saharan African restaurants in the country, crazy chef in the good way, but bring some African patience with you, as the waits can get rather long }} *{{eat | name=Tribeca NV | alt= | url=http://www.tribecanv.dk | email= | address=Bygmestervej 2 | lat=55.7061 | long=12.5344 | directions= | phone=+45 35 85 10 50 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=11:30-23:00 | price=Mains: 169 kr | content=This place is in a 1930s factory, and does a fairly good job combining a raw New York style loft - in the spirit of the neighborhood, with an Italian tratoria. And the food is excellent. }} * {{eat | name=Måltid | alt= | url=http://www.maltid.dk/ | email=mail@maltid.dk | address=Bispebjerg Torv 2 | lat=55.7159674 | long=12.5314736 | directions=next to Grundtvigskirken | phone=+45 35422224 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu-Fr: 11.00-22.00, Sa: 10.00-22.00, Su: 10.00-16.00 | price=DKK 155 for the daily special, DKK 350 for three course menu | content=Brunch, lunch and dinner in small six-tables restaurant with a view over Bispebjerg Square. The dining options in the northwest area are limited, but this is an excellent restaurant with an ambitious cook. There is plenty of fresh vegetables, high quality meat, chicken, and fish. }}
=== Splurge === * {{eat | name=Kiin Kiin | alt= | url=https://en.kiin.dk/ | email=kiin@kiin.dk | address=Guldberggsgade 21 | lat=55.691854 | long=12.557330 | directions= | phone=+45 35 35 75 55 | tollfree= | hours=Seatings from 16:30 to 19:30 | price=8 dishes tasting menu 1150 DKK, 7 glass accompanying wine menu 950 DKK | lastedit=2021-01-11 | content=Expensive, but takes Thai cuisine to a wholly different level, for which the restaurant was awarded its first Michellin Star in 2007. Book ahead on weekends as the restaurant fills up quickly. }}
==Drink== === Bars === * {{drink | name=Cafe Tjili Pop | url=http://www.tjili.dk/ | email= | address=Rantzausgade 28 A-B | lat=55.68555 | long=12.55222 | directions= | phone=+45 3535 9020 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Th 10:00-midnight, F Sa 10:00-03:00 | price=25 kr for beer | content= Small cozy cafe that gets up and going Fridays and Saturdays with live music and a lively, rocking crowd. Their live music quality is rather high, but is free entry. }} * {{drink | name=Blågårds Apotek | url=http://www.kroteket.dk | email= | address=Blågårds Plads 2 | lat=55.6864086 | long=12.5580131 | directions= | phone=+45 35 37 24 42 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=12:00-02:00 (winter from 15:00) | price= | content=This place is an institution on Nørrebro, and provides good atmosphere, propped up by candlelight and beer. There is Live music going on Thursday-Saturday }} * {{drink | name=Gilt | alt= | url=http://www.gilt.dk | email= | address=Rantzausgade 39 | lat=55.68706 | long=12.54784 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | hours=Tu 18:00-12:00, W-Sa 18:00-02:00 | price= | content=A bit far away from anything else, this is a good cocktail bar in unlikely surroundings. For Copenhagen the cocktails here are reasonably priced, and the servings are quite big. }} * {{drink | name=Saxons | url= | email= | address=Jagtvej 71 | lat=55.69395 | long=12.54816 | directions= | phone=+45 35 86 16 03 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Tu 11:00-midnight, W Th 11:00-03:00, F Sa 11:00-05:00, Su 11:00-23:00 | price= | content=Laid-back bar, which anonymous and little worn down looks, makes for a surprise when top-notch national and international DJs starts swinging the decks, in a nice mix of Hip Hop, Electronica and Dance music. Except for Wednesdays which are designated Reggae nights. }}
===Sankt Hans Torv nightlife=== [[Image:CPHsktHans.jpg|thumb|300px|Sankt Hans square]] Sankt Hans Torv is a square in the southern part of Nørrebro, the square itself, and the surrounding streets, is packed with fantastic bars and clubs - many locals go here to drink, and the close proximity of the bars make it a perfect place for a pub crawl, where you can breeze through the bars drink by drink, till you end up at a place you really enjoy. When it is warm Pussy Galore, Café Sebastopol and Plenum has very popular outdoor seating on the square. If you're cheap, just grab a beer in the 7-Eleven store on the corner, and hang out with the "poor" students by the fountain.
Sankt Hans Torv quarter '''bars''':
* {{drink | name=Barcelona | url=http://www.restaurantbarcelona.dk/ {{dead link|August 2020|December 2020}} | email= | address=Fælledvej 21 | lat=55.68986 | long=12.56034 | directions= | phone=+45 35 35 76 11 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-W 15:00-02:00, Th 15:00-04:00, F Sa 11:00-04:00 | price= | content=A large bar with a dancefloor, which also doubles as a restaurant during the day. It is very popular and gets crowded in the weekends - when the DJ starts spinning records around midnight. }} * {{drink | name=Cafeen Funke | url= | email= | address=Blegdamsvej 2 | lat=55.69080 | long=12.56140 | directions= | phone=+45 35 35 17 32 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Su-W 14:00-02:00, Th-Sa 14:00-05:00 | price= | content=The rather odd name, comes from a pun in Danish, translating into something like "Will the cafe work?". It is a cheap place, popular with the alternative crowd, and more of bar than a cafe. Sometimes there are live performances. }} * {{drink | name=Cafe Osbourne | url= | email= | address=Elmegade 23 | lat=55.69037 | long=12.55962 | directions= | phone=+45 35 39 99 17 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Su-Th 16:00-02:00, F 16:00-03:00, Sa 14:00-03:00 | price= | content=Are you favourite premier league club playing? this is your place then. Unpretentous to the extreme, and a few immigrant regulars who come here for a beer, chat and watching football. }} * {{drink | name=Cafe Sebastopol | url=http://www.sebastopol.dk | email= | address=Sankt Hans Torv 32 | lat=55.69093 | long=12.56031 | directions= | phone=+45 35 36 30 02 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-W 08:00-01:00, Th F 08:00-01:00, Sa 09:00-02:00, Su 09:00-01:00 | price=Brunch from 78 kr, mains from 128 kr | content=Huge café, with a large outdoor serving area, that gets very busy during sunshine - also serves decent food, so it is a good place for brunch if the weather is good. }} * {{drink | name=Mexi bar | url= | email= | address=Elmegade 27 | lat=55.69062 | long=12.55997 | directions= | phone=+45 35 37 77 66 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Th 19:00-02:00, F Sa 19:00-03:00 | price= | content=Tequila anyone? what other place to "get things moving", than probably the only Mexican themed bar in the city, on the corner of Elmegade and the square. It is quite cheap too. }} * {{drink | name=Oak room | url=http://www.oakroom.dk/ | email= | address=Birkegade 10 | lat=55.69064 | long=12.55829 | directions= | phone=+45 36 60 38 60 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu 19:00-midnight, W Th 19:00-02:00, F Sa 19:00-03:00 | price= | content=If you're up for some cocktails, head north past ''Rust'', and into this place in the basement. It is often really crammed, but take it as a sign that the cocktails here are good. Although there are a few misses on the drinks menu. }} * {{drink | name=Gefährlich | url=http://www.gefahrlich.dk {{dead link|December 2020}} | email= | address=Fælledvej 7 | lat=55.68891 | long=12.55981 | directions= | phone=+45 35 24 13 24 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu 17:00-01:00, W Th 17:00-03:00, F Sa 17:00-04:30 | price= | content=A seemingly odd mix of restaurant, cocktail bar, nightclub, hairdresser and art gallery, but it works surprisingly well. DJ starts spinning records for the crowd on the dance floor around 23:00 Tuesday to Saturday. }} * {{drink | name=Nørrebro Bryghus | url=http://www.norrebrobryghus.dk | email= | address=Ryesgade 3 | lat=55.69025 | long=12.56397 | directions= | phone=+45 33 30 05 30 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-W 11:00-midnight, Th-Sa 11:00-02:00, Su 11:00-22:00 | price= | content=This Brewpub is an excellent option for starting the night, with good food, and great beer, produces right in the tanks you are sitting between }} * {{drink | name=Plenum | alt= | url=http://www.cafeplenum.dk | email= | address=Sankt Hans Torv 3 | lat=55.69046 | long=12.56041 | directions= | phone=+45 35 37 02 77 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Th 10:00-midnight, F Sa 10:00-02:00, Su 10:00-22:00 | price= | content=Play it cool with the fashionable Danish students, from the medical faculty of the university. Outdoor seating on the sidewalk opposite the square, and good cakes! }} * {{drink | name=Pussy Galore's Flying Circus | alt= | url= | email= | address=Sankt Hans Torv 30 | lat=55.69104 | long=12.56057 | directions= | phone=+45 35 24 53 00 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 08:00-02:00, Sa Su 09:00-02:00 | price= | content= }} * {{drink | name=Temple Bar | url= | email= | address=Nørrebrogade 48 | lat=55.68957 | long=12.55656 | directions= | phone=+45 35 37 44 14 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Su-W 13:00-01:00, Th 13:00-03:00, F Sa 13:00-05:00 | price= | content=A nice down to earth place, with old 2nd hand sofas and lounge chairs on two floors, there is an equally worn out, but workable pool and fuss-ball table upstairs, and the speakers are playing non-mainstream tunes. }} * {{drink | name=Understellet | url= | email= | address=Nørrebrogade 24 | lat=55.68833 | long=12.55981 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Open to 05:00 | price=25 kr | content=Fancy a round of pool? this place is a bit worn down, but not only have two (worn out) pool tables and a game of fussball. It is also cheap, with beers going over the desk for }} * {{drink | name=Ølbaren | alt= | url=http://oelbaren.dk/ | email= | address=Elmegade 2 | lat=55.689370 | long=12.558000 | directions= | phone=+45 35 35 45 34 | tollfree= | hours=M 21:00-01:00, Tu-Sa 16:00-01:00 | price= | content=This is a small place, with one of the city's largest selections of beers. The menu is huge and very explanatory. If you're still lost - the clever staff will be able to sort you out. Crammed on weekends. }}
Sankt Hans Torv quarter '''clubs and venues''':
If you get tired of drinking, and want to burn out some calories on a dance floor, head for these two clubs. That also has frequent live acts on their stages.
* {{drink | name=Global Copenhagen | url=http://www.globalcph.dk | email= | address=Nørre Alle 7 | lat=55.69155 | long=12.56051 | directions= | phone=+45 30 64 00 33 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Underpinning the ethnicity of the neighborhood this nightclub and concert stage opened its doors in 2007. The stage hosts a variety of music from every corner of the globe, and on select evenings (check their website for details) Club Global takes over with DJs from every corner of the world. }} * {{drink | name=Rust | url=http://www.rust.dk | email= | address=Guldbergsgade 8 | lat=55.69125 | long=12.55928 | directions= | phone=+45 35 24 52 00 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=W-F 23:00-late | price=Cover charge for club: usually 60 kr | content=Rust is where most people head after 00:00 from the many bars above. It is one of the city's best clubs, but also a music stage for rhythmic music concerts several times per week (check website for program). }} * {{drink | name=Spillestedet Stengade | alt=Formerly known as Stengade 30 | url=http://www.stengade.dk | email= | address=Stengade 18 | lat=55.68821 | long=12.55566 | directions= | phone=+45 35 35 50 69 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=free - 90 kr | content=Located on Stengade this venue hosts concerts during the week and weekend, featuring a wide variety of genres including metal, rock, electronica, dubstep, jazz and hip hop, and hosts RUBA'DUB Sundays - "Copenhagens only weekly reggae bashment and the cities loudest Sunday party, every damn Sunday for the past 10 years." - every Sunday at 22:00 }}
==Sleep==
===Budget=== *{{sleep | name=Bellahøj Camping | url=http://www.bellahoj-camping.dk/ukpladsen.htm | email=info@bellahoj-camping.dk | address=Hvidkildevej 66 | lat=55.70151 | long=12.51341 | directions= | phone=+45 38 10 11 50 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=65 kr per person | checkin=24-hour reception | checkout= | content=As cheap as it gets, and as close as it gets to the city center for camping. The camping ground has a small shop, and rents out bikes for 65 kr/day. Only open in summer (July 1st-August 31st). }} *{{sleep | name=Bellahøj Youth Hostel | alt= | url=http://www.youth-hostel.dk/ | email=bellahoej@danhostel.dk | address=Herbergvejen 8 | lat=55.698750 | long=12.505267 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | checkin=14:00-17:00 | checkout=10:00 | price=140 kr for HI Hostel members | content=Nice clean hostel, with shared kitchen, TV lounge, laundry facilities and a foosball table available. The outdoor garden area is really nice, with barbecue, a mega size chess game, and benches available. Strict enforcement of no-alcohol policy though. }} * {{sleep | name=Sleep In Heaven Hostel | alt= | url=http://www.sleepinheaven.com | email=morefun@sleepinheaven.com | address=Struenseegade 7 | lat=55.68679 | long=12.55053 | directions= | phone=+45 35 35 46 48 | tollfree= | fax= | checkin= | checkout= | price=Beds starting from 130 kr in dorms | content=Right around the corner from Tjili Pop - a local bar. Pretty spartan conditions, except for the common area and toilets which have been renovated, but the only real backpackers style accommodation in town - along with the sidekick Sleep-in Green - with an outdoor beer garden, a pool table, free lockers and free internet. The staff are young and friendly and the place is clean. Alcohol from outside cannot be consumed inside the premises although you can buy beer and wine from the counter. }} * {{sleep | name=Urban Camper | alt= | url=https://www.urbancamper.dk/ | email=stay@urbancamper.dk | address=Lygten 2C | lat=55.70212 | long=12.5377 | directions=5 minute walk from metro, S-train and busses at Nørrebro Station | phone=+45 31 77 08 06 | tollfree= | checkin=15:00 | checkout=11:00 | price=115 DKK bed in 4-bed tent, 275 DKK private double tent, 420 DKK private 4-bed tent | lastedit=2021-01-11 | content=Indoor "camping" with tents set up in rows indoor. Chill lobby with 24/7 service, sandwiches and beer, fusball tables and pinball machines. Wifi, showers, lockers in tents, breakfast, linnen and towels are all included. Paid bike rental. }} * {{sleep | name=Globalhagen Hostel | alt= | url=https://www.globalhagen.dk/hostel | email=booking@globalhagen.dk | address=Ravnsborggade 11 | lat= | long= | directions=Nørreport St. metro | phone=+45 7731 0077 | tollfree= | checkin=15:00 | checkout=11:00 | price=e.g. Bed in six-bed dorm DKK 120/€15.40 | lastedit=2022-06-27 | content= }}
===Mid-range=== * {{sleep | name=Hotel Nora | url=http://www.hotelnora.dk | email=nora@hotelnora.dk | address=Nørrebrogade 18B | lat=55.6880668 | long=12.5604761 | directions= | phone=+45 35 37 20 21 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=Rooms from 850 kr | checkin= | checkout= | content=Nice location near the lakes and the good shopping and nightlife options in the district. It comes with free wireless internet and breakfast buffet. }}
==Connect== The postal code for Nørrebro is 2200 (''København N'') and 2400 (''København NV'') for the northwestern part.
* {{listing | name=Nørrebro Library | alt=Nørrebro bibliotek | url=http://www.bibliotek.kk.dk/english | email= | address=Bragesgade 8 | lat=55.6990193 | long=12.5436026 | directions= | phone=+45 35 85 68 53 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Th 11:00-19:00, F 11:00-17:00, Sa 10:00-14:00 | price=Free | content= }} * {{listing | name=Nørrebro Post office | alt=Nørrebro Posthus | url= | email= | address=Jagtvej 64 | lat=55.6957568 | long=12.5507702 | directions= | phone=+45 80 20 70 30 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 11:00-19:00, Sa 10:00-14:00 | price= | content=Offers the normal range of postal services, as well as Western Union transfer and selling tickets for concerts plays etc. }}
==Cope== * {{listing | name=Bispebjerg Hospital | url=http://www.bispebjerghospital.dk/topmenu/English/ | email=skadestue@bbh.regionh.dk | address=Bispebjerg Bakke 23 | lat=55.7145296 | long=12.5399550 | directions= | phone=+45 35 31 35 31 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=This hospital is the main ER for patients from the Nørrebro and [[Copenhagen/Østerbro|Østerbro]] districts. }} *{{drink | name=The Laundromat Cafe | url=http://www.thelaundromatcafe.com/ | email= | address=Elmegade 15 | lat=55.68998 | long=12.55863 | directions= | phone=+45 35 35 26 72 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Su-Th 08:00-midnight, F Sa 10:00-02:00 | price=32 kr for laundry including detergent | content=A combined bar/cafe and coin laundry, where you can wash your rags, drink a coffee or cold beer, and read in one of the numerous books, while you are waiting for you laundry to finish. }} * {{listing | name=Sjællandsgade Bad | url=http://sjaellandsgadebad.dk/ | email= | address=Sjællandsgade 12A | lat=55.69350 | long=12.55353 | directions= | phone=+45 35 39 06 06 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M 07:00-13:00, Tu-F 09:00-18:00, Sa 08:00-15:00 | price=Get a shower for 19 kr | content=Bath and spa. }}
{{guidedistrict}} {{geo|55.688333|12.559722}} {{IsPartOf|Copenhagen}}

revision.comment: /* See */ Updated listing for Sorgenfri Palace & Park
revision.text:
{{pagebanner|Northern suburbs of Copenhagen Wikivoyage banner.jpg|pgname=Northern suburbs of Copenhagen}}
The '''Northern suburbs''' is a green suburban district north of [[Copenhagen]]. The ghetto of the wealthy, the Whiskey belt; the nicknames are many, and the Northern suburbs is indeed home to most of the city's well-off population, but to casual visitors the difference may be subtle, due to the high income equality of Denmark and heavy taxation of the highest earners (up to 66% as of 2019). The district does however have more than its fair share of royal mansions, meticulously kept parks and golf courses. *{{marker|type=city|name =[[Ballerup]]|wikidata=Q257223}}, which has a charming museum of local history, is covered in a separate article.
== Understand == [[Image:Life guard tower, Klampenborg.jpg|thumb|300px|The Arne Jakobsen designed life guard tower on the Bellevue beach, is symbolic for the area]] It used to be heavily forested, a heritage still visible today with its many parks, lakes and forest. The area became a favourite excursion destination for city dwellers during the early days of Denmark's industrialisation, when trams and trains would be full of people going to the entertainment areas north of the city on Sundays and other holidays. Most of the places have since closed down, but the small but charming '''Dyrehavsbakken''' amusement park is the oldest still functioning in the world. The nearby horse racing tracks and surrounding forest continues to draw hordes of city folk on public holidays and weekends. A trip on the hundred-year-old tour '''Baadfarten''' ferries sailing on the lakes and canals near Lyngby will give you a taste of days gone by. These canals and lakes are remains of an elaborate defence for the city, where the canals would draw water of the lakes to flood the low-lying areas to thwart would-be invaders.
The industrialisation had another lasting impact on the district, as the earliest factories in the country shot up around the '''Mølleå River''' using water power to power the mills producing paper, weapons and textiles. But since steam power was introduced only a short time after these factories were built, development turned to back the city, and the area has been superbly preserved and is still surrounded by green forests, and amazingly most of old mills and factories still stand as they did when they were closed down in 19th century.
In the 1970s the old villages were joined together by huge patches of suburbia, the old village houses turning into residences for the wealthiest, and the suburbia occupied by the upper middle class, and bar a couple of minor exceptions, the area have become notoriously well off. The most interesting areas are without doubt the old Lyngby and along the Øresund coast, whereas the rest is mainly residential with little interest to travellers.
== Get in ==
{{infobox|Notes on geography|Locals tend to see this as a string of separate towns, usually called by the name of the nearest S-train station, but there wouldn't be much point in listing each of these separately in a travel guide, as each on its own has little to offer. Visitors should be aware though, that the suburban nature of this district means that the attractions are far between, and it could take up to an hour in public transportation traveling between Charlottenlund and Farum for instance, the names of the "towns" are listed behind the addresses in the listings}}
The area north of Copenhagen is served by 4 S-train lines, the western area which offer little in the way of attractions are served by lines H/C (towards Frederiksund) and A (Farum). The area to the east where the attractions are centred are served by lines E/B (Holte & Lyngby) and line C (Klampenborg) which runs parallel the coast. For access to the Dyrehaven forest and the amusement park it is also possible, and faster, to take regional ''kystbanen'' line (the coastal railway), as it has fewer stops - but be careful as some trains skip Klampenborg station.
All the S-train lines runs every 10 minutes throughout the day and every 20 minutes in the evening. the ''kystbanen'' runs every 6–14 minutes for most of the day, in the weekends and after 20:00, the service is cut back to every 20 minutes. It takes approximately 15 minutes to get to Hellerup and 20 minutes to get to Klampenborg and Lyngby, the 3 main attraction areas.
===By car=== There are two highways leading north of city '''Helsingørmotorvejen''' (route 19) starts on [[Copenhagen/Østerbro|Østerbro]], and runs north roughly halfway between the '''E'''/'''B''' (Holte) and '''C''' (Klampenborg) S-Train lines, just west of the Dyrehaven park to Lyngby and onwards to [[Helsingør]]. '''Hillerødmotorvejen''' (route 16) starts on the outer part of [[Copenhagen/Nørrebro|Nørrebro]] and runs north approximately halfway between the '''A''' (Farum) and '''H'''/'''C''' (Ballerup) S-Train lines to Farum, and onwards to [[Hillerød]]. '''Motorring 3''' is an orbital highway around Copenhagen, and connects to the two highways. '''Strandvejen''' runs all the way along the Øresund coast to Charlottenlund, Klampenborg and further north.
==Get around== [[Image:Lyngby-Holte.png|thumb|400px|'''Lyngby & Holte''' ''West: The area bordering the lake and riverland of the suburbs.'']] [[Image:Klampenborg-hellerup.png|thumb|400px|'''Klampenborg & Hellerup''' ''East: The area along the Øresund coast'']]
The center of Lyngby is walkable. For a physically fit visitor it is also possible to walk to ''Frilandsmuseet'' by following the main street. Alternatively to walk to ''Sophienholm'' or ''Dyrehavsbakken''. Though these are several kilometers away, you may walk in the green surroundings of the Dyrehaven forest, but it would much for feasible as an enjoyable bike trip. Unlike the central part of the city, it makes sense to have your own wheels in the northern suburbs, as some of the attractions, restaurants and hotels are a bit out of the way of bus transportation, which while perfectly feasible, has relatively infrequent departures in the district.
* Taxi. Taxis are usually lined up at the train stations of Klampenborg and Lyngby, until late in the evening. ** '''[https://taxa.dk/en/ Taxa 4x35]''', +45 35 35 35 35, is one of the local taxi companies in the area.
=== By train === The main mode of transport for locals is the S-trains, and while they are useful getting to and from the city center, they are not that useful to tourists as a way to get around, as they mainly cater to commuters going downtown.
'''Nærumbanen''' is a modern light rail line between ''Lyngby'' & ''Nærum'', but it wasn't always so. It was built to serve the booming industries along the Mølleå river, at the turn of the 20th century. Besides being the best mode of transport to reach Frilandsmuseet's eastern entrance, Brede Works (at Brede station) and the other mills along the river, it is quite scenic as it runs along the river in a forested area. It is colloquially known as ''Grisen'' or "the pig" in English, from the loud squeaky horn the old trains used to use frequently when crossing paths and roads.
=== By bus === In the Copenhagen suburbs, public transportation is built around the railway network. Almost every bus line begins at a Station, and will usually also end at one. Owing to Copenhagen's finger-based structure, transportation is centred on lines going to and from the inner city, and jumping between the fingers can be an unusually cumbersome. Below is a section of the most useful buslines in the area.
* '''169''' & '''179''' runs the same ring route, but in opposite directions. And will get you between ''Lyngby'' & ''Charlottenlund'' stations, but it is rather slow and it may be quicker to take the S-trains to Hellerup and change to the '''C''' line to go back north. * '''191''' Is the only option to get to the canoe rental places, and sights in ''Nybro'' and ''Frederiksdal''. It runs twice an hour most of the day between Lyngby and Sorgenfri station. * '''388''' is the best option for transportation between the two main attraction areas of ''Lyngby'' and ''Klampenborg'', it stops at both stations, and runs along the border of ''Dyrehaven'', but remember to check the schedule as it is fairly infrequent with only 2 departures per hour. * '''400S''' Is the best option between ''Lyngby'', ''Farum'' and ''Ballerup''. There is no direct connection to Farum, so you'll have to switch to the S-train in at Bagsværd station, and take the train onwards to Farum, or stay on the bus to go to Ballerup.
=== By boat === '''[http://baadfarten.dk/ Baadfarten]''' has been sailing passengers on the Mølleå canal, and on Furesø, Bagsværd and Lyngby lakes, in their famous and charming white boats, for more than 100 years. Besides being an excellent way to see the sights in Frederiksdal and Nybro, it is a popular excursion in itself. It is the oldest of the 6 ferries sailing; ''Princes Alexandrine'' and ''Prince Christian'' were built in between 1896-1896, and thus dating back to the beginning. It used to be driven by steam but has been modified several times and now uses a diesel engine.
The usual starting point is from the pier in Lyngby, not to far from the station and departs about every 40 minutes between 10:00-17:00 during summer (early June - mid August), from May to the end of September there is also services on weekends on the stretch between Frederiksdal and Sorgenfrivej. There are 3 regular routes.
* Lyngby - Nybro - Bagsværd * Lyngby - Nybro - Frederiksdal * Frederiksdal - Furesøbad (Farum)
On Sundays there are also an additional service connecting Frederiksdal, Birkerød and Holte on the Furesø lake. Tickets are between 35-40 kr, and a full day ticket can be bought for 110 kr. The routes are plotted on the map.

The sights in this district is mainly suitable for warm summer days, and for visitors with a historical interest, but if those two prerequisites are met a visit here can be incredibly rewarding. Bring your bike on the train though, or bring your patience as the main attractions are located far apart, and public transportation links in the area leaves a bit to be desired. And in the not unlikely event of poor weather, there is still a world-class art museum to browse through without getting wet.
[[Image:Friland.jpg|thumb|House on Frilandsmuseet]]
* {{see | name=Brede Værk | alt=Brede Works | url=https://natmus.dk/museerne/brede-vaerk/| email= | address=I.C. Modewegs Vej, Lyngby | lat=55.7947 | long=12.4992 | directions=Nærumbanen railway to Brede - interchange with S-trains at Jægersborg Station | phone=+45 33 13 44 11 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Sundays during summer 12:00-14:00 | price=Free | wikipedia=Brede Works | image=Brede Værk set fra haven.jpg | wikidata=Q4960125 | content=Guided tours of this complex from industrialization of Denmark. It is a rare display of a preserved full industrial complex from the 18th century, with both the works, homes of workers and leaders, the dining hall, garden and the rich factory owners home to put it all in context. Easily combined with a visit to Frilandsmuseet. }} * {{see | name=Frilandsmuseet | alt=Open Air museum | url=http://en.natmus.dk/museums/frilandsmuseet/ | email= | address=Kongevejen 100, Lyngby | lat=55.7857 | long=12.4899 | directions=Bus 184 from Lyngby Station to the Frilandsmuseet stop | phone=+45 33 47 38 55 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu-Su 10:00-17:00 | price= 65 kr (free for under 18 years) | wikipedia=Frilandsmuseet | image=Copenhagen open air museum.jpg | wikidata=Q1150616 | lastedit=2016-08-21 | content=This open-air museum covers a huge green area between Kongevejen and the Mølleå river, in fact it is one of the world's largest museums in terms of area covered. It houses more than 50 authentic relocated historical farms, houses, and windmills, and sometimes live activities like making butter are performed inside the houses. If your feet get sore, there are horse drawn carriages to take you around for a fee. Guided tours during Sundays. There are also some good picnic spots and a restaurant. }} * {{see | name=Furesø Museums | alt=Furesø Lake museums of local history, Farum | url=http://www.furesoemuseer.dk | email= | address=Stavnsholtvej 3 | lat=55.815137 | long=12.403012 | directions=Walk (10 minutes) from Farum station | phone=+45 72 35 61 00 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu-F 11:00-16:00, Su 13:00-16:00 | price=Free | content=This is a string of 4 museums housed in old historic buildings, 3 of them in the small village of Stavsholt. There is one permanent exhibition on ''Stavnsholtvej 170'', which is the immigration museum, showcasing stories about the lives of individual immigrants through the times. The other 3 properties centers around local history, and have shifting art exhibitions as well as local history. }} * {{see | name=Gammel Holtegård | alt= | url=http://glholtegaard.dk/da/ | email= | address=Attemosevej 170, Holte | lat=55.8289 | long=12.5175 | directions=Bus 193 from Holte Station to Gammel Holtegaard stop | phone=+45 45 80 08 78 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu-F 11:00-16:00, Sa Su 11:00-17:00 | price=60 kr | wikipedia=Gammel Holtegård | image=Gammel Holtegaard 2011.jpg | wikidata=Q5520347 | content=18th-century palace turned exhibition space, with many good art exhibitions usually on schedule (check their website). It is a good idea to call ahead, and get a tour since its openings hours seems a bit random. }} * {{see | name=Mindelunden | alt=Ryvangen Memorial Park | url=http://www.mindelundenryvangen.dk | email= | address=Tuborgvej 67, Hellerup | lat=55.7257 | long=12.567083 | directions=Take bus 21 from Hellerup station to the bus stop named Estersvej | phone=+45 39 62 14 67 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Jan-Apr 10:00-16:00, May-Aug 10:00-19:00, Sep-Jan 10:00-16:00 | price=Free | wikipedia=Ryvangen Memorial Park | image=Mindelunden i Ryvangen - grave-1.jpg | wikidata=Q3359393 | content=Mindelunden is a memorial park in Hellerup where the Danish freedom fighters who died in World War II are buried. * {{see | name=Sophienholm | alt=Lyngby | url=http://www.sophienholm.dk | email= | address=Nybrovej 401 | lat=55.7754 | long=12.4576 | directions=Bus 191 from Lyngby Station to Sophienholm stop | phone=+45 45 88 40 07 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu Th-Su 11:00-17:00, W 11:00-20:00 | price=Admission to the art museum: 45 kr, garden is free | wikipedia=Sophienholm | image=Sofienholm (Lyngby).jpg | wikidata=Q3432506 | content=It is an old manor with a nice view over the lakes. There is a restaurant, a small art museum and there is a large garden with an outdoor stage. A nice alternative to the directions given above, is to take the baadfarten ferry's during the summer to the museums own pier. }} * {{see | name=Sorgenfri Palace & Park | alt= | url=http://kongehuset.dk/en/palaces/sorgenfri-palace | email=kommunikation@ses.dk | address=Kongevejen 8, Lyngby | lat=55.778756 | long=12.496894 | directions=Bus 184 from Lyngby Station to Sorgenfri Slot stop | phone= | tollfree= | hours=Always open | price=Free | wikipedia=Sorgenfri Palace | image=Sorgenfri.jpg | wikidata=Q1968159 | content=While the inside of this palace and its inner garden is not open to the public, since it is one of the active royal properties, it still lends grace to the surrounding park, which is quite pleasant, and a good place for a stop if you are touring Mølleåen river. }} * {{see | name=Brønshøj | alt= | url= | email= | address= | lat=55.7042 | long=12.4982 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Brønshøj | image=Brønshøj Bibliotek 09-04-06.jpg | wikidata=Q3354065 | lastedit=2019-09-07 | content=The suburb Brønshøj is a village dating back to the early middle ages. It is reached by bus 2A, 5A or 350S all stopping at Brønshøj Torv (square). Close by the square is the church from the 12th century, the oldest building still in use is Copenhagen. Brønshøj served as the head quarter for the Swedish army during the attack on Copenhagen in 1659. Many street names between Brønshøj Torv and Bellahøj are named in memory of the war (Svenskelejren - 'the Swedish camp' and Svend Gønges vej). As of Sep 2019, Brønshøj Museum is under reconstruction. }}

== Do == [[Image:Rollercoaster -1 Bakken.jpg|thumb|200px|The old wooden roller coaster on Dyrehavsbakken]] [[Image:Cph-Hubertus.jpg|thumb|200px|The annual Hubertus hunt is a great spectacle these days.]]
* {{do | name=Bellevue Theatre | url=http://bellevueteatret.dk | email= | address=Strandvejen 451, Klampenborg | lat=55.777524 | long=12.590101 | directions=Walk 5 minutes from Klampenborg station | phone=+45 39 63 64 00 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Ticket office hours: M-F 15:00-18:00 | price=Prices varies, but if you are 4 people under 25, tickets are 60 kr a piece. | content=Stage by the Øresund coast, with a wide range of performances, from international ballet companies over comedy to conventional plays. Lists international performances, relevant for foreign visitors, on the English version of their website. }} * {{do | name=Dyrehaven | alt=Officially Jægersborg Dyrehave and literally in English The Deer Garden | url= | email= | address= | lat=55.791389 | long=12.560833 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Jægersborg Dyrehave | image=Jægersborg Dyrehave - Hermitage Pavilion and deer.jpg | wikidata=Q1714967 | content=A forest park north of Copenhagen. It covers around 11 km². The northern boundary is at Jægersborg Hegn (Forest), which until 1832 was part of Dyrehaven. Eremitageslottet is a small royal hunting castle placed on a hilltop in the northern part of the area. There are several event's taking place here throughout the year, The ''[http://www.hubertusjagt.dk Hubertus hunt]'' the 1st Sunday of November is a huge sporting event with hundreds of "hunters" in red jackets on horseback. While the [http://www.elob.dk erimetage running event], the largest in the country, takes place on the 1st Sunday of October. There are also sometimes theatrical performances in the Ulvedalene (Valley of wolves, do not worry they are long gone) - check with a tourist information for details. }} * {{do | name=Ride on a pony in Dyrehaven | alt= | url=https://www.fortunensponycenter.com/rideture {{dead link|December 2020}} | email= | address=Ved Fortunen 33 | lat=55.7755509 | long=12.5438829 | directions=At Fortunen restaurant | phone=+45 45876058 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Summer M-F 12:00-18:00; Sa Su holidays 09:00-18:00; winter M Tu Th 09:00-12:00, F 12:00-sundown | price=From 120 kr | lastedit=2016-02-14 | content=Rent a pony to ride in Dyrehaven. If you have small children, you can rent tiny ponies, that the children can ride, while you are walking the ponies. }} * {{do | name=Dyrehavsbakken | alt=colloquially known simply as Bakken | url=http://www.dyrehavsbakken.dk | email= | address=Dyrehavevej 62, Klampenborg | lat=55.775816 | long=12.575086 | directions=Part of the experience is walking from Klampenborg Station through the beautiful park, or take a horsedrawn carriage from the station | phone=+45 39 63 35 44 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=25-Mar-29 Aug; hours vary but generally 14:00-22:00 are safe bets | price=Entrance is free. Wristband for all running amusements: 199-219 kr, discount booklets with 42 coupons: 210 kr | content=The world's oldest intact surviving amusement park, and has a reputation for being more folksy than its downtown rival, Tivoli. While the number of rides are hardly impressive, except for the large wooden "Rutchebanen" (lit. the rollercoaster) which dominates the park, the atmosphere it gets from the surrounding forest is quite enchanting, and the many old and very traditional pubs and restaurants is the real attraction of the place. }} * {{do | name=Experimentarium | alt=Science center | url=http://www.experimentarium.dk | email= | address=Tuborg Havnevej 7, Hellerup | lat=55.726613 | long=12.57923 | directions=Bus 1A from downtown or Svanemøllen Station to Tuborg Boulevard stop | phone=+45 39 27 33 33 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M W-F 09:30-17:00, Tu 09:30-21:00, Sa Su 11:00-17:00 | price=135 kr for adults, 90 kr for children | content=Excellent "hands-on" science museum for kids of all ages. Rich with activities centered around nature and technology, and does wonders teaching complex subject easily through self experimentation. Has changing themed exhibitions (check website for current ones). }} * {{do | name=Klampenborg Galopbane | alt=Copenhagen racecourse | url=http://www.galopbane.dk | email= | address=Klampenborgvej 52 | lat=55.770843 | long=12.56763 | directions=Bus 388 from Klampenborg Station to Krathusvej stop | phone=+45 39 96 02 13 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=One of the most beautiful gallop runways in the world. Right at the border, and with great views over, the Dyrehaven park. A few restaurants, a grill with excellent fresh grilled sausages, etc., and a picnic area. When there are races on Fridays, it is a good place to go for some beers while watching the races. }}
{{infobox|Paddling down Mølleå river|Mølleåen is small 30 km river, cutting through the northern suburbs of Copenhagen, and offers a unique urban escape, flowing almost entirely through quite forests and parks, but also entirely within the Greater Copenhagen city proper.
'''Prepare'''
It is a good idea to call one of the rental places ahead as there is regulations in place limiting the number of canoes available for rent, it is also mandated to have life jackets for everyone on the canoe - your rental place will supply these for free. It is a good idea to wear rubber boots, flip flops or other footwear that can stand getting wet. Remember to bring plenty of liquids, snacks and food as there are few places to stuck up supplies on route, and remember to bring one of the brochures with a map available for free at the rental place. Since you have to carry the canoe over a few roads at the old dams - you might want to shell out the 20 Kr extra for a set of wheels for the canoe.
'''Route'''
After Fursø lake, right next to '''Frederiksdal's canoe rental''' outfit, the first stop is the old Frederiksdal mill, the romantic yellow brick buildings, was a paper mill build in 1880. If this is your starting point, you will be paddling down the fortification canal; a part of a string of canals that were designed to flood the low lying areas north of Copenhagen, with water from Furesø lake if Copenhagen ever was to become besieged. On the northern side of the canal, Lyngby marshes is a protected area due to the unique natural environment. After crossing the large Lyngby lake and sailing under the train tracks, you will have to transport the canoe over a piece of land past the cute yellow '''Lyngby Mills'''.
Heading north you will have a nice view over '''Sorgenfri Palace''' on your left, before another carry at Fuglevad. Onwards you will sail along the '''Open Air Museum''' on your left, and a local railway on your right, which used to carry workers to the mills. Next stop is Brede with the impressive '''Brede Works''' towering above the dammed lake. Here you once again need to carry the Canoe over the dam. Next dams (and carries) is at '''Ørholm''' and '''Nymølle''', the long red building in Ørholm dates back to 1886, and along with mill in Nymølle, it was the centre of the Danish paper industry in the early days of Industrialisation. The forest towering on both sides of the river is nicknamed ''The Danish Switzerland'' due to the unusually hilly and steep terrain.
Just before reaching the Motorway bridge, the first sleeping option is the commercial camping grounds of '''Nærum Camping''' due north of the river. Continuing you will have around 30 minutes of sailing, before reaching the free rudimentary camping grounds of '''Stampen''' start looking for a parking lot on your right, a couple of bends after the water treatment plant. Onwards there is a short carry over Svenskevej road, before you reach the idyllic village of '''Raadvad''' in the middle of the '''Dyrehaven''' forest. Another industrial centre with an old inn that offers excellent dining (see Rådvad Kro), and a secluded hostel.
From here it is smooth sailing through the beautiful park or forest, until you reach '''Strandmøllen''', a picturesque mill, and the only of the old mills that still houses production. Just north of the damn is another rudimentary camping ground, and right after the mill, you have reached the end of your journey, the water turns salty, as you have reached the Øresund coast.
'''Sleep'''
While it’s possible to sail the entire route in a day if you’re physically fit and hurried, It's possible to overnight at several locations along the river, the offers range from primitive free campgrounds to hostels, and allow you to discover the river at more leisurely pace. * '''Furesøens Teltplads''' – Primitive free campgrounds with a bonfire space. * '''Nærum Campingplads''' see entry in sleep section * '''Stampens Teltplads''' - Primitive free campgrounds with a bonfire space. * '''Rådvad Vandrehjem''' see entry in sleep section * '''Strandmøllens Teltplads''' Primitive free campgrounds with a bonfire space and running water }}
===Mølleå River===
The small river Mølleåen (literally ''Mill river'') runs through the center of Lyngby near the church. It is possible to follow the river on foot or bike from Lyngby all the way to the sea (Øresund) through park and forest. But an even better option is to rent a Canoe or Kayak from one of places below and sail down the estuary from Frederiksdal or Nybro near Lyngby to the sea in Øresund.
* {{do | name=Nybro Båd & Kanoudlejning | alt= | url=http://www.nybrokano.dk/ | email= | address=Nybrovej 384, Holte | lat=55.774621 | long=12.468973 | directions=Bus 191 from Lyngby station to Nybro Kro stop | phone=+45 45 87 39 19 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=100 kr/hour, two days from 500 kr, several discounts available | content=Has both row boat, canoe and kayak rental during the summertime. offers pickup and delivery service for a minimum fee of 500 kr. This place is in the middle of the canal connecting Furesø and Lyngby sø lakes. }} * {{do | name=Frederiksdal kano & bådudlejning | url=http://www.frdal.dk | email= | address=Nybrovej 520, Lyngby | lat=55.780741 | long=12.446437 | directions=Bus 191 from Lyngby Station to Frederiksdal stop | phone=+45 45 85 67 70 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu-F 10:00-18:00, Sa Su 09:30-18:00 | price=100 kr/hour, multiday trips from 200 kr/day, several discount packages available | content=Canoes, kayaks and row boat rentals, This is the outlet closest to Furesøen, Sophienholm and Hotel Frederiksdal. Also has pick up and delivery of Canoes for a minimum of 500 kr. }} * {{do | name=Havnehytten | alt= | url=http://havne-hytten.dk/ | email= | address=Rustenborgvej 21, Lyngby | lat=55.773470 | long=12.493317 | directions=Walk 15 minutes from Lyngby Station | phone=+45 45 93 93 69 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=10:00-19:00| price=100 kr/hour or 380 kr for full day | content=This place poses a bit of dilemma, while it is by far the easiest place to get to with public transportation, since it is just a short walk from the S-train station, they do not offer pick up services if you plan to do longer trips. Also doubles as a restaurant. }}
=== Beaches === While the fancy new beaches in the inner harbour of [[Copenhagen/Indre By|downtown]] Copenhagen, are definitely more unique and conveniently located - The beaches along the Øresund coast dotted north along the Strandvejen road, following the coast, are where Copenhagerners used to make their escape from hot summer days in the city. And still on warm summer days you see hordes of cyclists making their way north, with towels sticking out their bags, as the inner city "beaches" doesn't quite offer the great green surroundings the northern beaches does.
* {{do | name=Bellevue Beach | alt= | url=https://www.visitdenmark.com/denmark/bellevue-beach-gdk482349 | email= | address=Strandvejen 340, Klampenborg | lat=55.778 | long=12.5923 | directions=Walk 4 minutes from Klampenborg station | phone=+45 39 90 06 95 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Bellevue Beach | image=Bellevue Beach (Copenhagen).jpg | wikidata=Q3375957 | content=Copenhagen's own Copacabana, and probably the country's most famous beach, with half a million visitors per year. Wide (but packed on sunny days) sandy beach and a large grassy area. Toilets, ice cream kiosk, beach volleyball fields, and 3 piers. }} * {{do | name=Charlottenlund Fortress | alt= | url=http://www.gentoftekommune.dk/charlottenlundfort | email= | address=Strandvejen 144, Charlottenlund | lat=55.745 | long=12.587 | directions=Bus 14 from Svanemøllen Station | phone=+45 39 62 36 88 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Charlottenlund Beach Park | image=Charlottenlund Søbad aerial.jpg | wikidata=Q15209172 | content=Nice beach with a huge grassy area for oozing in the sun, and a narrow strip of sand before the calm waves of Øresund. There is also a large pier with better facilities ([http://www.charlottenlund-soebad.dk Charlottenlund Søbad]) but it has a 20 kr entrance fee. The old fortress behind the beach has a camping ground (see sleep section). }} * {{do | name=Furesøbad | url=https://marina-en.dk/ | email= | address=Frederiksborgvej 101, Farum | lat=55.80340 | long=12.38661 | directions=Bus 500S from Værløse station to Fiskebæk kroen, or the Baadfarten ferries | phone=+45 20 10 02 90 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=An artificial sand beach and marina in the Furesø lake, there are lifeguards stationed here during the season, and the restaurant has great view over the lake, and is a good option for a brunch if you are in the area. Also a small kiosk for ice cream, etc., and a pier for the baadfarten ferries. }} * {{do | name=Frederiksdal Fribad | alt= | url=http://fribad.dk/ | email= | address=Frederiksdal Badesti 1, Virum | lat=55.785002 | long=12.443706 | directions=Bus 191 from Lyngby Station to Furesø Parken stop | phone=+45 22 34 71 71 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=Free | content=A cozy sand beach near the canoe rental place in Frederiksdal. It is kid friendly with lifeguards keeping watch. There is both shallow water for the kids, and a pier out to the deeper water in the Furesø Lake. Excellent spot for a picnic on the grassy areas and there is also a restaurant (''see listing in eat section'') and ice cream shop. }} * {{do | name=Hellerup Strandpark | url= | email= | address=Strandparksvej, Hellerup | lat=55.73272 | long=12.582296 | directions=Bus 1A from downtown or Hellerup Station to Hellerupvej | phone=+45 39 98 81 10 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=This small beach,marina and park is hidden away at the end of a residential street of Strandvejen street. While the beach here is not as good as the other beaches in the area, it is less crowded and closer to the city. The small marina is open to visiting yachts, and there is cluster of Tennis courts right behind it. }}
=== Events & festivals === * {{do | name=The Hubertus hunt | alt= | url=http://www.hubertusjagt.dk/ | email= | address= | lat= | long= | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=A modern continuation of the traditional par force hunts that has been held in Dyrehaven park for hundreds of years, but the game have now been replaced by two riders wearing fox tails on their backs. It is an annual spectacle with almost 30,000 spectators watching the 50-odd riders wearing fancy deep red jackets, ride through a challenging course. Most of the spectators head straight for the ''Magasin'' dam, where the riders pass through a water obstacle, and 5-10 riders usually makes an involuntary but spectacular plunge into the freezing water. Takes place in late October or early November, contact a tourist information desk for details. }} * {{do | name=The Erimitage run | alt= | url=http://www.elob.dk/ | email= | address= | lat= | long= | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=The world's oldest fun run, where 19,000 runners challenge themselves on a 13.3-km course through the beautiful Dyrehaven park, and have done so every year in early October since 1969. If you plan to attend it is a good idea to reserve a spot ahead as there is cap of 19,000 participants for logistical reasons and considerations for the wildlife in the park, Send an email to ''elob@ultimate.dk'' to secure yourself a place on the starting line. }} * '''Midsummer night by Lyngby lake''' Midsummer night, is known in Denmark as ''Sankt Hans aften'' (St. John's Eve) and takes place on June 23 every year. It is celebrated with large bonfires to ward away evil spirits, which has been tradition since the Viking age. A more modern addition is to put a straw witch on the bonfire, and send her back to Bloksbjerg (a mountain in Germany). The bonfire at Folkets Park ''(by Viggo Stuckenbergs Vej)'' facing Lyngby Lake, is one of the most popular in the area, with many spectators, speeches and musical performances.
[[Image:Cph molleaa.jpg|thumb|250px|A couple quietly paddling down the Mølleå river in early spring.]]

==Eat== Northern Copenhagen offers some stellar options for dining. While the listings below can be a bit cumbersome to get to, they have been selected because they offer a unique atmosphere special to the district, many back from the day when the area was covered by forest and dotted by small villages. Check their websites and you will discover that most of these restaurants are old inns, housed in white chalked buildings, thatched roofs, and green surroundings. It is certainly not cheap - even though basic dishes on many of the listings below often hovers around 150 kr, expect the final bill to be much higher. Even then, the quality food, and unique environments, should make the extra cost and transportation time worthwhile. * {{Eat|name=Cafe Jorden Rundt|url=https://www.cafejordenrundt.dk|address=Strandvejen 152, Charlottenlund|lat=55.74980|long=12.58936|directions=On Strandvejen, north of Hellerup, and just north of Charlottenlund fort.|phone=+45 39637381|hours=09:00-20:00|price=DKK 130 mains|lastedit=2022-01-13|content=You cannot miss this characteristic round cafe cafe with the copper roof. The food is typical cafe fare, but done very well. You can eat with a great view of Øresund or the Chartlottenlund forest on the other side. You can bring your dog if it is not wet, so do not let it swim in Øresund just before you eat. Easy parking}} * {{eat | name=Brede Spisehus | alt= | url=https://www.bredespisehus.dk/en/ | email= | address=I.C. Modewegsvej 1, Lyngby | lat=55.794197 | long=12.500478 | directions=Nærumbanen railway to Brede - interchange with S-trains at Jægersborg Station | phone=+45 45 85 54 57 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu-Sa 11:30-23:00, Su 11:30-15:00 | price=Set menu from 265-335 kr, and main courses from 198 kr | content=This restaurant close to Brede værk and the open air museum, was the original canteen for the workers at the mill factory, and has been serving food in the current house for more than 100 years - and still has the gas lamps and a beautiful glass ceiling as it did when it was built. In good weather there is outdoor serving, of the French-inspired menu, with view over the picturesque lake. }} * {{eat | name=Den Gule Cottage | url=http://www.dengulecottage.dk | email= | address=Strandvejen 506, Klampenborg | lat=55.779676 | long=12.590407 | directions=walk 5 minutes from Klampenborg Station | phone=+45 39 64 06 91 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu-Sa 12:00-14:00 & 16:00-23:00 | price=Set menus from 465 kr | content=This tiny restaurant is superbly located on a large patch of grass, between the blue Øresund sea and the green Dyrehave park, in an idyllic small yellow half timbered house with thatched roof, which used to work as porter's lodge for the surrounding entertainment area in the 19th century. The chef is trained in France which shows on the menu - which also shows connection with the nature surrounding the restaurant. Not much space, and few tables, so book ahead. }} * {{eat | name=Frilandsmuseets Restaurant | alt= | url=http://www.frilands-restaurant.dk | email= | address=Kongevejen 100, Lyngby | lat=55.785458 | long=12.490074 | directions=Bus 184 from Lyngby Station to the Frilandsmuseet stop | phone=+45 45 85 34 80 | tollfree= | hours=Tu-Su 10:00-17:00 - only in season. | price= | content=The restaurant is in connection with the open air museum, and is a convenient location for a brunch or lunch if you are visiting the museum, and the setting is quite beautiful. Has gotten some harsh review on their service though. }} * {{eat | name=Nybro Kro | url=http://www.nybrokro.dk | email= | address=Nybrovej 376, Lyngby | lat=55.774992 | long=12.466473 | directions=Bus 191 from Lyngby station to Nybro Kro stop | phone=+45 45 87 01 49 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=12:00-21:00 | price=Mains from 245 kr | content=A very traditional inn from the turn of the 20th century, now only functioning as a restaurant, with white chalked walls and thatched roof. It' possible to sit outside and eat in the garden. Menu and crowd is as Danish as they come. Good option if you have been canoeing as it is very close to the Nybro Canoe rental place. }} * {{eat | name=Peter Liep's Hus | url=http://www.peterliep.dk | email= | address=Dyrehaven 8, Klampenborg | lat=55.777540 | long=12.577275 | directions=Walk or take a horse carriage from Klampenborg Station | phone=+45 39 64 07 86 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=10:00-18:00 and until midnight during dyrehavsbakken's season | price=Mains from 108 kr | content=This charming old white building, is in the middle of the forest right near the entrance to ''Dyrehavsbakken'', It has been serving food to the guest of the forest since 1888, and takes its name from the hunter who opened it back in the days. Middle of the road cuisine, but the deer - fresh from the surrounding forest, is recommended. }} * {{eat | name=Restaurant Charlottenlund Fort | url=http://charlottenlundfortet.dk/ | email= | address=Strandvejen 150, Charlottenlund | lat=55.744827 | long=12.586212 | directions=Bus 14 from downtown or Svanemøllen Station to Constantia stop | phone=+45 39 62 22 63 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content= }} * {{eat | name=Restauranten ved Frederiksdal Fribad | url=http://www.fribad.dk | email= | address=Frederiksdal Badesti 1, Lyngby | lat=55.785313 | long=12.444565 | directions=Bus 191 from Lyngby Station to Frederiksdal stop | phone=+45 45 83 81 85 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=17:00-22:00 | price=All you can eat buffet 198 kr | content=A hangout for locals in nice weather, is this restaurant located right next to the lake bath of the same name. While there is a few tables indoors, the real attraction is the outdoor tables by the lake, where you can dine till you drop with food from the barbecue. }} * {{eat | name=Rådvad Kro | url=http://www.raadvadkro.dk | email= | address=Svenskevej 52, Lyngby | lat=55.806708 | long=12.559433 | directions=Bus 182 or 183 from Lyngby Station to Rådvad stop (very few buses per day) | phone=+45 45 80 61 62 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu-Sa 12:00-15:00 & 18:00-21:00, Su 10:00-15:00 | price=Mains from 125 kr | content=Almost running on its 150th year, this old inn is outstandingly located, near one of the old mills on Mølleå river, in the middle of the forest. The menu is modern - yet classic - Danish cooking, with the menu changing along with the seasons. Offers cooking courses if you arrange this ahead. }} * {{eat | name=Kongekilden | url=http://www.kongekilden.dk | email= | address=Enghavevej 8, Klampenborg | lat=55.772135 | long=12.560361 | directions=Bus 388 from Klampenborg Station to Røde port stop | phone=+45 39 63 31 01 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=W-Su 12:00-15:00 | price=Brunch 128 kr, Lunch platter 168 kr | content=A lunch restaurant in the Dyrehaven park, it is housed in charming red brick building dating back to the 18th century, which used to be the home of a forest ranger. Traditional Danish lunch with rye bread, salmon and cheese as well as some excellent cakes. }} * {{eat | name=Sankt Peder | alt= | url=http://sankt-peder.dk/ | email= | address=Sankt Peders Vej 2, Hellerup | lat=55.730767 | long=12.5757475 | directions= | phone=+45 39612275 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Sa 11:30-21:00; 11:30-21:00 | price= | lastedit=2016-02-14 | content=Very traditional Danish lunch restaurant (smørrebrød), but they are open all day. Can be busy so reservations are recommended, but usually they will find you a seat soon anyways. Good place to have drink too. And in the summer you can sit outside. }} * {{eat | name=Hellerup Sejlklub | alt= | url=http://www.restauranthellerupsejlklub.dk/ | email=restauranths@gmail.com | address=Strandparkvej 36, Hellerup | lat=55.731268 | long=12.5809649 | directions= | phone=+45 39628803 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Apr 1-Oct 9 12:00-15:00, 17:30-21:30; 12:00-15:00 in the winter. | price= 100 kr | lastedit=2016-02-14 | content=This is the restaurant in Hellerup Sailing Club. But non-members are welcome (although some tables are reservered for club member). You get excellent food at reasonable prices and in the summer you can enjoy the spectacular view over Øresund from the rooftop terrace. }}
===Splurge=== * {{eat | name=Søllerød Kro | url=http://www.soelleroed-kro.dk | email= | address=Søllerødvej 35, Holte | lat=55.81353 | long=12.495258 | directions=Bus 195 from Holte Station to Søllerød Kro stop | phone=+45 45 80 22 70 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu-Su 12:00-15:00, 18:00-22:00 | price=Mains from 295 kr | content=This is another old inn, turned restaurant. A romantic setting on the edge of a forest, by a lovely old church and village pond, topped of with a view over a small palace.While expensive, it is one of the top restaurants in Copenhagen, and has a shiny star in the Michelin guide to prove it. One the most extensive wine menus in the country, and the service is usually top quality. }}
===Ice cream===
The suburbs north of Copenhagen have many great ice cream stores. The most well-known is probably Lydolph's Isbar on Strandvejen ''(Beach Road)'' which has big ice creams and long queues in the summertime. There are also many Paradis outlets in this district.
* {{eat | name=Lydolph's Isbar | url= | email= | address=Strandvejen 167, Hellerup | lat=55.73266 | long=12.57537 | directions= | phone= +45 3962 7747 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Open from March to September | price= | content= }} This is probably the most well-known ice cream store in this district. It is located near Øresund and often attract long queues in the summertime. They are known for their large ice creams including the Lydolpher which is the largest of them. On the first day in the season they give away free soft ice.
* {{eat | name=Paradis | url= | email= | address=Hellerup, Gentofte, Lyngby and Søborg | lat= | long= | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content= }} Paradis is a chain of ice cream stores in Denmark. Unlike the most ice cream stores the ice cream is made naturally and sometimes has some special flavours. The ice cream can be bought in either a cone or a tub, but also in large boxes. The store also offers milkshakes made of ice cream.
==Drink== If you headed north of downtown to drink in this district, you would be heading in the opposite direction of everyone else who crowd the late trains, to head for the nightlife in the center - or to put it a bit more bluntly, do not! If you are based in the Northern suburbs there are night buses once an hour on weekends to take bring you home safely from the watering holes of the inner districts. However, usually, in weekends and on Thursdays, many events and parties are held various places on the '''Technical University of Denmark''' (DTU) campus.
Another option in the summer is to head in the direction of '''Dyrehavsbakken''' (''see listing in the ''do'' section''), or stay there for eating if you're in the area. There are quite a lot of options; '''Ølgod''' is a Tyrolean style bierhalle, a bit tacky, but a potential for a lot of fun if you do not take yourself to seriously. '''Bakkens Hvile''' is a veritable institution in Danish culture, where the Bakken girls have been entertaining the crowd with singing, flirting and dancing in Danish cabaret style for as long as anyone can remember. Any of the other options are easy to find on weekends, when the crowd can get a bit rowdy, but usually in an innocent and hugely entertaining way.
* {{drink | name=Post Pub | url=http://www.post-pub.dk | email= | address=Lyngby Hovedgade 78 | lat=55.7689224 | long=12.5053408 | directions= | phone=+45 45 88 64 00 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-Th 11:00-00:00, F Sa 11:00-01:00, Su 11:00-22:00 | price=No cover charge | content= }} * {{drink | name=Templet | url=http://www.templet.dk | email= | address=Jernbanevej 16 | lat=55.7701386 | long=12.49961 | directions= | phone=+45 45 87 44 97 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=From 50 kr and upwards | content=Often live music Friday and Saturday. }} *{{drink | name=Club Stahlwerk | url=http://www.club-stahlwerk.dk | email= | address=Brønshøjvej 6, Brønshøj | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+45 38 81 66 22 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=23:00-05:00, but no regular schedule (check website) | price=Usually 50 Kr | content=A club for alternative music in genre's like synthpop, dark wave, and industrial, in the basement of a cafe. Only open when events are taking place, so check dates on their website (in English). }}
==Sleep==
=== Budget===
* {{sleep | name=Rådvad Vandrehjem | alt=Danhostel Lyngby Vandrehjem | url=https://lyngbyhostel.dk/ | email= | address=Rådvad 1 | lat=55.805584 | long=12.560002 | directions=Bus 182 or 183 from Lyngby station stops here a few times every day. | phone=+45 45 80 30 74 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=Dorm beds 125 kr, rooms from 390 kr | checkin= | checkout= | content=Really out of the way place in the middle of the forest, on the Mølleå river, in a house which used to be a boarding school dating back to 1917. If you really want to escape the rush, this idyllic village is a good option. You better have a canoe and your own wheels handy though, as the bus only goes here 5 times a day. Otherwise it is a 20 minute walk through the forest to the nearest bus stop that sees regular traffic. }}
=== Mid-range ===

Also houses a rather good à la carte restaurant. }} * {{sleep | name=Hotel Fortunen | url=http://www.fortunen.dk | email= | address=Ved Fortunen 33 | lat=55.775098 | long=12.544557 | directions=Bus 388 from Klampenborg station to Ermelundsvej stop | phone=+45 45 87 00 73 (no email) | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=Rooms from 425 kr with shared bathroom | checkin= | checkout= | content=A small hotel, in charming yellow brick building from the 1930s. it is at the entrance the huge Dyrehaven park, and many rooms have windows facing the park, with an old fashioned 30s feel - you'll be the judge whether that is good or bad (pictures on the website). Also doubles as a solid traditional restaurant. }} * {{sleep | name=Hotel Postgaarden | url=http://www.post-pub.dk | email=info@postpub.dk | address=Gammel Jernbanevej 18, Lyngby | lat=55.768864 | long=12.505274 | directions=Walk 5 minutes from Lyngby station | phone=+45 88 10 22 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=Singles with shared bathroom from 425 kr | checkin=14:00 | checkout=11:00 | content=Cheap hotel in central Lyngby, all rooms either share bathroom with other rooms in the wing, or 2 rooms share one in the new wing. Nice views over the large park on the property - you can even arrange a picnic with row boat in the dam. Also home of a nice restaurant. }}
=== Splurge ===

{{pagebanner|Vesterbro Hummleby Jerichausgade Wikivoyage banner.png|pgname=Vesterbro of Copenhagen|caption=Detail of the frontage of Jerichausgade in Hummleby in Vesterbro}} '''Vesterbro''' is just outside [[Copenhagen]]’s city centre )the Inner City or [[Copenhagen/Indre By|Indre By]]), making it a very attractive place to live. It includes the districts of '''Sydhavnen'''. '''Enghave''', '''Valby''' and '''Vanløse'''.
[[Image:VesterbroTorv.jpg|thumb|Vesterbro square]]
The district is west of the city center, at the site of the old Western Gate ("Vesterport"), which once served as an entrance to the old city. Along with the other three gates — Østerport ("Eastern Gate"), near the current Østerport Train Station; Nørreport ("Northern Gate"), near the present-day Nørreport Train Station; and Amagerport ("Amager Gate" as the southern gate), between Christianshavn and the island of Amager—Vesterport, which was dismantled in 1856.
The name "Vesterbro" can be misleading, when translated directly into modern Danish as "Western Bridge"; it does not refer to an actual bridge, but rather to the paved ("brolagt") road that led into the city through the Western Gate.
Over the years, Vesterbro has undergone significant renovation, transforming it into one of the most central and desirable places to live. Previously, the area was known for issues such as prostitution and drug trafficking, making it a place where only the poorest resided. While some of these activities still persist, particularly on Istedgade and near Halmtorvet, increased police efforts have been focused on improving safety and cleanliness in the district.
For more details, visit our [https://kebabish.dk/ website].
